title: Resolve blobSeen=false in identity-less dashboard capture
description:
Parent: bf-cmoze (split). Depends on bf-11qvm (SyntaxError fix). After the JS parses cleanly, the identity-less capture still shows blobSeen=false / renderEvidence=null (see identity-less.live.json under docs/notes/). SIM_RATE already defaults to 30 in scripts/run-sim-dashboard-console.sh (the known 20->30 flake fix is applied), so this is NOT the SIM_RATE 0-blob flake. The likely cause was the SyntaxErrors aborting the render path; once those are fixed, determine whether a blob now appears.

SCOPE:
- Re-run scripts/run-sim-dashboard-console.sh (no --ble, identity-less phase).
- If blobSeen is still false after bf-11qvm, inspect the DOM/render path: confirm whether a blob element is created at all, or whether it appears outside the capture window (timing/seed).
- If a config bump (e.g. SIM_DURATION, SIM_SEED, blob-timeout) is the fix, set it in the script or env and record why in a comment.

ACCEPTANCE:
- [ ] identity-less .live.console.txt summary line shows blobSeen=true.
- [ ] identity-less.live.json renderEvidence is non-null (fallback color observed) OR a blob element is confirmed in the DOM.
- [ ] If a config/seed change was needed, recorded in a comment on this bead.

title: Land the IO-6 hard-gate test behind a build tag so go test ./... stays green
description:
Split from bf-5312 (IO-6 hard-gate scenario + triage gate). Child 1 of 4 — the unblocker. Depends on nothing.

WHY bf-5312 FAILED 3x: NEEDLE runs `cd mothership && go test ./...` (no -short) on every bead close (.needle.yaml:42,67). The IO-6 hard-gate test TestIO6HardGate_WalkerProducesTrackedBlob currently lives in mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go with NO build tag, so the default suite picks it up. It is correctly strict and therefore RED (zero tracked blobs) until upstream bf-4q5w wires fusion Engine.SetNodePosition — so every close attempt fails NEEDLE's mandatory test run. The ~217 IO-6 lines are also currently UNCOMMITTED in the working tree.

SCOPE (do NOT weaken the assertion — it stays byte-for-byte strict):
- Move the IO-6-only code out of e2e_test.go into a NEW file mothership/tests/e2e/io6_gate_test.go with a `//go:build io6_gate` build constraint as the FIRST line. IO-6-only symbols (confirmed used nowhere else in the e2e dir): TestIO6HardGate_WalkerProducesTrackedBlob, CaptureIO6Diagnostics, io6RunMaxima, GetNodeRecords, GetStatus, StatusResponse.
- Leave the SHARED helpers (GetBlobCount, GetEvents, WatchDashboardWS, AssertBlobObserved, AssertDetectionEventsObserved, AssertDuringRun, RunSimulator) in e2e_test.go / assertions_test.go — UNTAGGED — because TestFullE2EIntegration and TestDetectionEvents reuse them.
- The assertion body is unchanged: still t.Fatalf on zero blobs, still NO skip-on-zero-blobs, still NO log-downgrade.
- Commit the move.
- Document the gated invocation as a runbook comment at the top of io6_gate_test.go: `cd mothership && go test -tags io6_gate -run TestIO6HardGate ./tests/e2e/...`

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `cd mothership && go test ./...` is GREEN (IO-6 gate excluded by the build tag — this is what unblocks NEEDLE closure for the sibling e2e work).
- `cd mothership && go vet ./...` is clean.
- `cd mothership && go test -tags io6_gate ./tests/e2e/...` COMPILES (it is expected to FAIL/RED until bf-4q5w; child 2 verifies the RED state).
- The assertion in TestIO6HardGate_WalkerProducesTrackedBlob is unchanged (strict).
- The change is committed.

title: Fix 5 dashboard JS SyntaxErrors surfaced once assets serve correct MIME
description:
Parent: bf-cmoze (split). The findDashboardDir() MIME fix (bf-2qywd) made dashboard JS execute for the first time. The re-captured console under docs/notes/ (file identity-less.live.console.txt, captured 2026-07-08) now shows 5 pageerror SyntaxError lines with empty stacks: Unexpected token close-paren, Unexpected token function, Unexpected identifier systemHealth, Invalid left-hand side in assignment, Unexpected token close-paren. These appear IDENTICALLY in both the identity and identity-less captures (both pageErrors=5), so they are pre-existing dashboard JS parse errors, NOT a backward-compat regression. systemHealth is referenced in dashboard/js/app.js and dashboard/js/ambient_renderer.js.

SCOPE:
- Locate source file(s)/line(s). Empty stacks mean run node --check on each dashboard/js/*.js to find unparseable files, or load each module in a headless browser with source info.
- Fix each so every dashboard/js/*.js parses cleanly.
- Do NOT touch data/API JSON handlers or identity-render guards (out of scope; see bf-2gmx audit).

ACCEPTANCE:
- [ ] node --check passes for every file under dashboard/js/.
- [ ] Re-run scripts/run-sim-dashboard-console.sh; the identity-less .live.console.txt has 0 pageerror SyntaxError lines.
- [ ] No dashboard module-load regressions vs the current capture.

title: Document the working --ble matcher recipe in notes/hardware-free-runtime.md
description:
## Objective
Split from parent bf-1yr1w. Once child 2 makes `run-sim-ble-match.sh` PASS, capture the working `--ble` opt-in + params in `notes/hardware-free-runtime.md` so later beads (the parent capstone bf-2m534 / `scripts/run-sim-identity.sh`) reuse rather than re-derive. This closes the bf-1yr1w acceptance gap: the `--ble` opt-in is currently documented only for the FIXTURE (`run-sim-ble-fixture.sh` via `notes/ble-identity-fixture.md`), NOT for the matcher script.

## Depends on
Child 2 (`run-sim-ble-match.sh` must PASS first).

## Context (reuse, don't re-derive)
- `notes/hardware-free-runtime.md` already documents `run-sim-local.sh` and (in its "For later beads" section) the `--ble` + BLE-registry REST pairing. The matcher script `scripts/run-sim-ble-match.sh` and its params (`SIM_WALKERS=2 SIM_RATE=30 SIM_DURATION=25 SIM_SEED=42`) are NOT yet recorded there — add them.
- The fixture half is already documented in `notes/ble-identity-fixture.md` — link to it, do not duplicate.
- `run-sim-ble-match.sh` observes via `GET /api/ble/matches` (`identityMatcher.GetAllMatches`); a PASS record is a real blob match (`blob_id != -1`, `person_name=Alice`, `confidence >= 0.6`).

## Scope
- Confirm `bash scripts/run-sim-ble-match.sh` still PASSes at HEAD (re-run it; capture the PASS line + final `/api/ble/matches` summary as evidence).
- Add a "BLE identity matcher (--ble)" subsection to `notes/hardware-free-runtime.md`: the script, its env params, the `/api/ble/matches` observation surface, and the exact PASS line / evidence from this run.
- Link `notes/ble-identity-fixture.md` for the fixture half.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `run-sim-ble-match.sh` re-run at HEAD prints PASS (real blob match for Alice, `blob_id != -1`, `confidence >= 0.6`).
- [ ] `notes/hardware-free-runtime.md` records the `--ble` matcher script + params + observation surface + PASS evidence (closes bf-1yr1w criterion 3).
- [ ] The change reuses/links the fixture note rather than duplicating it.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Documented the working --ble matcher recipe in notes/hardware-free-runtime.md (new 'BLE identity matcher (--ble)' subsection): the script, the sim --ble params, the SIM_WALKERS=1 gotcha (NOT 2 — second walker's blob is ~1.73m off, kills matchConf <0.60), the /api/ble/matches observation surface (identityMatcher.GetAllMatches), and PASS evidence at HEAD 7757149 (blob_id=7, person_name=Alice, confidence=0.714, is_ble_only=false). Links notes/ble-identity-fixture.md for the fixture half rather than duplicating. Also committed scripts/run-sim-ble-match.sh (child-2 artifact, was untracked) so the recipe reproduces at HEAD. Verified: bash scripts/run-sim-ble-match.sh -> PASS exit 0; go vet ./mothership/... clean. Commit c42989e pushed to main.

title: Spatial quick actions (Component 32)
description:
## Goal
Right-click (desktop) or long-press (mobile) anywhere in 3D view to get context-sensitive actions based on what's under cursor.

## Scope
Actions per target:
- On blob: 'Who is this?' (BLE assignment), 'Why is this here?' (explainability), 'Follow' (camera tracks), 'Create automation here', 'Mark incorrect' (thumbs-down), 'Track history'
- On node: 'Diagnostics' (CSI plot), 'Blink LED', 'Reposition', 'Update firmware', 'Show links', 'Disable/Enable'
- On empty floor space: 'What happened here?' (filter timeline), 'Add trigger zone', 'Add virtual node', 'Coverage quality'
- On zone label: 'Zone history', 'Edit zone', 'Create automation', 'Crowd flow'
- On portal: 'Crossing log', 'Edit portal', 'Reverse direction'
- On trigger volume: 'Edit trigger', 'Test', 'View log', 'Disable/Enable'

## Implementation
Three.js Raycaster determines what's under cursor
Single context menu component renders appropriate options
Each action dispatches to existing dashboard functions (no new backend endpoints needed)

## Acceptance
- Right-click/long-press on blob shows blob-specific actions
- Right-click/long-press on node shows node-specific actions
- Right-click/long-press on empty space shows space-specific actions
- Actions execute correctly
- Works on both desktop (right-click) and mobile (long-press)

title: Build and document the BLE identity fixture (registered person + sim device)
description:
## Objective
Decomposed from parent bf-2m534 (acceptance capstone: observe non-empty identity on a runtime blob). This is the FIRST link — establish the BLE fixture that the matcher needs to produce ANY identity match. Without a registered person bound to the sim's advertised device, the matcher has nothing to match and identity stays empty regardless of the wire fix.

## Context (reuse, don't re-derive)
- The sim's --ble flag exists (mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:56) and sendBLEMessages (main.go:1125) advertises each walker as device addr AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:%02X (walker.ID), name sim-person-%d (main.go:1142-1144). With --walkers 2 --seed 42 the advertised addrs are walker-id-derived — confirm which addr(s) actually appear.
- REST flow exists (internal/ble/handler.go): POST /api/people to register a person; POST /api/ble/devices/preregister and/or PUT /api/ble/devices/{mac} to bind a device addr to that person. GET /api/ble/devices and GET /api/people to verify.
- Base env: scripts/run-sim-local.sh (see notes/hardware-free-runtime.md) starts a clean mothership (fresh mktemp data dir, 127.0.0.1:8088). The fixture must be applied to THAT running instance.

## Scope
- Start a mothership via scripts/run-sim-local.sh env (or a minimal equivalent) on a fresh data dir.
- Register one person via POST /api/people.
- Bind the sim's advertised device addr (AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:%02X for the relevant walker) to that person via preregister/PUT.
- Verify via GET /api/ble/devices that the device is associated to the seeded person (person_name/person_id populated).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A documented, repeatable REST sequence registers a person AND binds the sim's AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:%02X device addr to that person.
- [ ] GET /api/ble/devices on a running mothership confirms the device is associated to the seeded person (person_id/name non-empty).
- [ ] The exact REST calls + the observed sim BLE addr convention are recorded in a note (extend notes/hardware-free-runtime.md 'For later beads' section, or a sibling note) — reuse, not re-derive.
- [ ] If preregister/PUT cannot bind the sim device addr (e.g. device must be seen-live first), state that explicitly with evidence and document the working alternative.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Already done — closing to break the stuck-open loop (auto-split fired at failure-count:3 because prior br close --body attempts failed, not because the work was incomplete).

Deliverables (now committed 0caf5ef): scripts/run-sim-ble-fixture.sh (canonical, self-verifying fixture) + notes/ble-identity-fixture.md (reuse-note). All four acceptance criteria met and verified 2026-07-08 at HEAD 36b9729:

[1] Documented repeatable REST sequence registers a person AND binds the sim device: POST /api/ble/devices/preregister -> POST /api/people -> PUT /api/ble/devices/{mac}. YES.
[2] GET /api/ble/devices confirms the device is associated to the seeded person: ./scripts/run-sim-ble-fixture.sh PASSes both phases, exit 0. phaseA: AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:00 -> person_id=e7f179bd-... person_name=Alice (NO live adv). phaseB: same, binding survives live sim --ble advs.
[3] REST calls + sim BLE addr convention recorded in note: AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:%02X per walker (ids from 0), so --walkers 1 advertises AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:00.
[4] Seen-live-first? NO — PreregisterDevice sets last_seen_at=now so the device falls inside the GetDevicesSeenInHours window with no live sighting; and ProcessRelayMessage upserts never touch person_id, so the binding survives live advs.

NOT split — work was verifiably complete (branch b: implemented-but-uncommitted -> commit + close).

title: Fix automation-builder.js invalid-LHS assignment parse error
description:
Parent: bf-11qvm (split). Scope: make dashboard/js/automation-builder.js parse cleanly.

ROOT CAUSE (already located): automation-builder.js:295
    document.getElementById('trigger-cooldown')?.value = trigger?.cooldown || 60;
Optional chaining on the LEFT-hand side of an assignment is a SyntaxError
("Invalid left-hand side in assignment"). This is the matching pageerror.

FIX: resolve the element to a local first, then guard (match surrounding var/const style):
    const _cd = document.getElementById('trigger-cooldown');
    if (_cd) _cd.value = trigger?.cooldown || 60;
Behavior identical: set cooldown only when the field exists.

OUT OF SCOPE: other files; data/API handlers; identity-render guards.

ACCEPTANCE:
- [ ] node --check dashboard/js/automation-builder.js passes.
- [ ] Trigger-editor cooldown still populates from trigger.cooldown when the field exists; no-op when absent (same intent).

title: Build spaxel:0.1.354, deploy to ardenone-cluster, re-flash ESP32
description:
After bf-49fp (Dockerfile cache-bust fix) is merged:

1. Bump VERSION file to 0.1.354
2. Push to git to trigger spaxel-build CI on iad-ci
3. Monitor workflow until ronaldraygun/spaxel:0.1.354 is pushed
4. In ~/declarative-config, update the spaxel Deployment image tag to 0.1.354
5. Push declarative-config, ArgoCD syncs (Recreate strategy, ~30s downtime expected)
6. Verify mothership is healthy at spaxel.ardenone.com/healthz

Then re-flash the ESP32:
- Navigate to spaxel.ardenone.com provision page
- Connect ESP32-S3 via USB
- Run flash — mothership will now serve the 4MB firmware (no more crash loop)

Success criteria: ESP32 boots, starts captive portal or connects to WiFi, no reboot loop.
CI submit cmd: kubectl --kubeconfig=~/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig create -f - (spaxel-build workflow)

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Cannot complete as written — bead is stale. (1) VERSION REGRESSION: targets 0.1.354 but repo is at 0.1.357 and deployed mothership at spaxel.ardenone.com/healthz reports 0.1.358 (healthy, ~7.9d uptime); bumping to 0.1.354 would be a downgrade. (2) PREREQUISITE SATISFIED: bf-49fp is CLOSED, merged as commit 301a588 (cache-bust / firmware crash-loop fix) and already deployed in 0.1.358. (3) PHYSICAL STEP UNREACHABLE: ESP32 re-flash needs a human to connect the ESP32-S3 via USB and flash via the browser provision page (Web Serial). No serial device attached (/dev/ttyUSB*, /dev/ttyACM* absent, no USB-serial in lsusb), no firmware .bin in repo, esptool not installed. Deploy intent (get crash-loop fix built and live) is already done by 0.1.357/0.1.358; only the physical reflash remains, which needs a human + connected hardware. Leaving OPEN per retry guidance.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: RETRY VERIFICATION (2026-07-02T18:56:56Z): Task is stale and structurally impossible to execute as written. (1) VERSION is 0.1.357 locally; task wants 0.1.354 = a downgrade. (2) Deployed mothership spaxel.ardenone.com/healthz = {status:ok, version:0.1.358, uptime_s:683554 (~7.9d), db:ok, shedding_level:0} -- healthy, NOT crash-looping. (3) Prereq bf-49fp (Kaniko cache-bust fix) is CLOSED, merged as 301a588, already shipped in 0.1.358. The crash-loop fix this chain exists to deliver is already built, deployed, and stable. (4) Only remaining step = physical ESP32-S3 USB re-flash via browser Web Serial, which is unreachable from a shell: no /dev/ttyUSB* or /dev/ttyACM*, no esptool installed, no release spaxel-*.bin in repo. Closing as SUPERSEDED; no code/cluster changes made. Sole outstanding item = manual physical reflash against the already-deployed 0.1.358.

title: Capture end-to-end runtime logs and confirm zero undefined-field errors
description:
## Objective
Decomposed from parent bf-f841 (final step). Run the full identity-blob scenario end-to-end and produce a captured log artifact proving there are NO runtime errors from undefined/missing identity fields — the explicit goal of the parent's last acceptance criterion.

## Scope
- Run the complete runtime scenario established by the prior steps (blob created + identity populated + projections fed).
- Capture the runtime log / console output to an artifact (file or quoted log block).
- Grep/scan the captured log for panic, nil-pointer dereference, or any identity/undefined-field error.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Full scenario runs to completion without the process crashing.
- [ ] A captured runtime log artifact is referenced in the bead.
- [ ] Scan of the log finds zero panic / nil-deref / undefined-field errors related to identity fields.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Auto-split dispatch fired (failure-count:3) but the work is verifiably COMPLETE — closing instead of splitting.

All three acceptance criteria independently verified against the real captured log:

1. Ran to completion without crashing — script exit 0; full 10-step graceful shutdown present (mothership.log: 'Received signal interrupt' -> [SHUTDOWN] Step 1/10..10/10).
2. Captured runtime log artifact — committed in c858ffc at docs/notes/bf-15oi-runtime-capture/ (mothership.log 2414 lines, sim.log, identity_blob.json, SUMMARY.md). The run-sim-identity.sh CAPTURE_DIR hook is also part of this commit.
3. Zero identity-field errors — scanned the real mothership.log: panic=0, nil-pointer=0, runtime/fatal/SIGSEGV=0, undefined/missing-field=0, [ERROR]=0, [FATAL]=0, identity-lines-that-are-warn/error=0. sim.log: 0. Log levels 2340 INFO / 10 DEBUG / 10 WARN (benign hw-free noise: missing dashboard dir, pre-bind health race, token-migration window, fleet degraded at shutdown) / 0 ERROR / 0 FATAL.

Evidence blob (identity_blob.json, blob 7) carries fully-populated identity: personName=Alice, person_id=576aab22..., person_label=Alice, person_color=#f97316, identity_confidence=0.714, identity_source=ble, identityResolved=true.

Note: the 3 'failures' were 10-min agent timeouts (exit 124) AFTER the run+capture were already done — the prior runs produced the artifacts but never reached br close. Not a scope problem; no split needed.

Sibling-bead in-flight edits to mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go and mothership/internal/ble/identity.go were left uncommitted — not this bead's work.

title: Strengthen TestDetectionEvents to assert at least one detection event
description:
Split from bf-5jeo (verification capstone). Depends on bf-2330 (assertion primitives). Child 2 of 4.

CONTEXT: mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go:679 TestDetectionEvents currently only checks events != nil. The comment says "The endpoint must return a valid (possibly empty) events list" and t.Logf("Events API functional: found %d detection events", ...) — it explicitly ducks the count. This is one of the dodges called out in bf-5jeo.

SCOPE:
- Replace the nil-only check with a hard assertion that len(events.Events) >= 1, using the helper added in bf-2330.
- Provide an actionable failure message (include count observed + that the fusion pipeline produced no blobs).
- Keep the existing 4-node / 2-walker / 15s RunSimulator config and the timeout handling.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- TestDetectionEvents asserts >= 1 detection event (no longer accepts an empty list as pass).
- Failure message names the likely upstream cause (fusion/tracking produced no blobs).
- Test compiles (go build ./tests/e2e/...).
- NOTE: this test is expected to be RED until upstream bf-4q5w (fusion SetNodePosition wiring) lands — that strictness is the entire point of this capstone. Do NOT weaken the assertion to make it green.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Implemented: replaced TestDetectionEvents' nil-only events check with a hard assertion that len(events.Events) >= 1, using the AssertDetectionEventsObserved helper from bf-2330. On failure it now t.Fatalf's with an actionable message including the count observed and the likely upstream cause (fusion/tracking produced no blobs from the 4-node/2-walker CSI stream). Preserved the existing 4-node / 2-walker / 20 Hz / 15s RunSimulator config and the duration+2s timeout handling. Verified: go build ./tests/e2e/... OK, go vet ./... clean, assertion-helper unit tests pass. Deliberately strict — expected RED until upstream bf-4q5w (fusion SetNodePosition wiring) lands; assertion was NOT weakened. Commit a8d0759 pushed to main.

title: Observe >=1 blob via /api/blobs and record reproducible sim params
description:
Split from bf-51fq (umbrella). BLOCKED on the fix bead (bf-2eub9). This IS the original bf-51fq acceptance — achievable once the fusion pipeline emits blobs.

## Task
While spaxel-sim streams, observe at least one tracked blob at runtime via GET http://localhost:8080/api/blobs (JSON array of tracked blobs), or via /ws/dashboard snapshot/delta carrying a blobs array. Record the working sim parameters so the result is reproducible.

## Scope
- Use blob_observation.sh in the repo root. It is already corrected: the sim -duration flag is int-seconds (not "40s"); it binds port 8088 to avoid the old 8080 squatter.
- Known COSMETIC bug (does NOT affect the blob metric): the nodes_online jq filter select(.status=="online") always reads 0 because /api/nodes has no status field (it uses role/last_seen_at). Nodes DO connect (visible in sim log / fleet coverage 0->21%). Fix or ignore.
- If 0 blobs after ~30s, tune the env overrides (SIM_NODES, SIM_WALKERS, SIM_RATE, SIM_DURATION, SIM_SEED) until >=1 appears. Defaults: nodes=4 walkers=2 rate=20 duration=40 seed=42.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least one blob observed via /api/blobs (or /ws/dashboard) during a run.
- [ ] Working sim parameters (nodes, walkers, rate, duration, seed) recorded in a comment so the result is reproducible.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
VERIFIED at HEAD 231d756 — >=1 blob observed via /api/blobs. Closing; not splitting.

## Why the 27 "failures" were not real
All 27 prior dispatches were exit_code=124 (outcome:timeout, duration 600001ms) — the agent hit the 10-min ceiling every run. The bead was also BLOCKED on bf-2eub9 (umbrella) -> bf-1r1ya -> bf-243os, which all landed at HEAD:
  b9f362c fix(bf-561zr): split colon-joined LinkID so sim CSI reaches Fuse
  231d756 fix(bf-243os): strip Fuse debug instrumentation; complete runtime sim->blob path
Both bf-2eub9 and bf-243os are closed. The runtime sim->blob path is complete; the work is now trivially satisfiable, so splitting would re-do already-working code.

## Evidence (two independent checks at HEAD)
Binaries include the fix (built 21:43 Jul 7, ~1.5h after fix landed 20:17; fusion.go debug grep clean).

1) Authoritative sim --verify mode (mothership/cmd/sim built at HEAD, queries GET /api/blobs 12x@500ms, asserts blob_count == walker_count +/-1):
   --nodes 4 --walkers 2 --rate 30 --duration 15 --seed 42 --verify
     -> [SIM] PASS: 2 blobs detected for 2 walkers  (1/2 walkers have a blob within 2m)
   --nodes 4 --walkers 4 --rate 30 --duration 15 --seed 42 --verify
     -> [SIM] PASS: 4 blobs detected for 4 walkers

2) blob_observation.sh (the script in this bead's scope, polls /api/blobs 1/s over the window):
   SIM_NODES=4 SIM_WALKERS=4 SIM_RATE=30 SIM_DURATION=25 SIM_SEED=42 ./blob_observation.sh
     -> peak_blob_count=2  first_blob_at=10s  fps=363.4  reject_in_sim_log=0  nodes_online=4
     -> [bf-51fq] PASS: at least one blob observed via /api/blobs

## Reproducible params (recorded per acceptance criterion 2)
- Script:   SIM_NODES=4 SIM_WALKERS=4 SIM_RATE=30 SIM_DURATION=25 SIM_SEED=42 ./blob_observation.sh
- Verify:   ./sim --mothership ws://localhost:8088/ws/node --nodes 4 --walkers 2 --rate 30 --duration 15 --seed 42 --verify
- Key lever is SIM_RATE=30 (vs default 20): denser CSI lets fusion peaks clear the DeltaRMS threshold reliably. With defaults (rate=20, walkers=2) the result is nondeterministic (the prior run once hit peak=2; my default re-run hit 0) because 2 walkers don't always move enough within the window.

## Acceptance
- [x] At least one blob observed via /api/blobs during a run (peak_blob_count=2).
- [x] Working sim params recorded above (rate=30 reproduces reliably via both the script and --verify).

title: CI: add idf.py test --target linux firmware gate to spaxel-build WorkflowTemplate
description: After bf-31bp writes host-based firmware test targets (nvs/csi/serial_prov), wire them into the spaxel-build WorkflowTemplate in declarative-config as a step before the Docker build. firmware/test/ directory does not yet exist and CMakeLists has no host-test targets.

title: Seed realistic node geometry in spaxel-sim default placement
description:
Split from bf-4q5w (parent umbrella). Depends on the runtime-propagation child.

Even with positions wired, the spaxel-sim default virtual-node placement leaves nodes co-located / at origin, so the Fresnel excess path length |P-T|+|P-R|-|T-R| collapses toward 0 everywhere and the accumulation grid has no meaningful peaks, producing no blobs (the core symptom in bf-4q5w).

Scope:
- In the simulator virtual-node creation path (createVirtualNodes and the simulator package, e.g. internal/simulator/registry_bridge.go where PosX/PosY/PosZ are sourced from node.Position), give the default virtual nodes a realistic spread-out geometry across a default zone instead of co-located/origin positions.
- Ensure these positions flow into both the registry and the fusion engine via the wiring from prior children.
- Pick a sane default room/zone size consistent with roomWidth/roomDepth used in main.go for the localization system.

Acceptance criteria:
- Default sim nodes occupy a spread, non-degenerate geometry (not all at origin, not co-located).
- A sim or host test shows the fusion accumulation grid produces non-zero peaks with the default placement.
- Existing simulator tests still pass; go build ./... passes.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Closed as complete — verified the full default-placement chain.

bf-18yn is the umbrella; its scope was implemented by the closed children bf-xrej (impl) and bf-1kid (acceptance test), both already on origin/main.

Verification (all green):

title: Fix mothership static-asset MIME types so the dashboard executes
description:
## Objective
Root-cause fix unblocking parent bf-4do5y (live dashboard backward-compat) and grandparent bf-2gmx. The prior bf-4do5y captures are a FALSE PASS: every captured console shows blobSeen=false and every /css/*.css refused as text/plain (not a supported stylesheet MIME) and every /js/*.js refused execution under strict MIME checking — so NO dashboard JavaScript ever ran, no blob rendered, and the "0 page errors / 0 identity hits" verdict is meaningless. Evidence is in the existing console-capture logs under docs/notes/ for this bead (the .live and .ambient captures show the CSS/JS MIME-refusal lines).

## Scope
- Locate the dashboard static-asset handler in the mothership Go code (the one serving /css, /js, /static, index.html on the dashboard port).
- Ensure correct Content-Type is set by file extension: .css to text/css, .js to text/javascript (or application/javascript), .html to text/html, .svg to image/svg+xml, .json to application/json, .png/.jpg/.ico to the appropriate image types. Prefer mime.TypeByExtension or net/http FileServer/ServeFile over a custom Write that leaves Content-Type unset (some custom handlers default the unset body to text/plain).
- Do NOT change the data/API handlers (application/json) or the floorplan image handler.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] curl -sI of /js/app.js returns Content-Type text/javascript (or application/javascript)
- [ ] curl -sI of /css/tokens.css returns Content-Type text/css
- [ ] go build + existing go tests pass; no handler regressions
- [ ] Brief comment on bf-4do5y noting the root cause + fix (file:line)

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE FOUND (empirically verified) — the bead's original premise is a MISDIAGNOSIS.

The dashboard static handler does NOT leave Content-Type unset. It uses http.ServeFile, which sets correct MIME via mime.TypeByExtension. The real problem: the handler is never REGISTERED on the host, so every asset 404s.

REPRO (binary /tmp/spaxel-mothership, exact env from scripts/run-sim-dashboard-console.sh):
- cfg.StaticDir defaults to '/dashboard' (a container path). The harness does NOT set SPAXEL_STATIC_DIR, so on the host /dashboard is missing.
- mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go ~4601-4624 (filesystem-fallback branch): os.Stat(cfg.StaticDir) fails -> logs '[WARN] Dashboard directory not found: /dashboard' -> the r.Get("/*", http.ServeFile) handler is NEVER registered, and there is NO fallback to findDashboardDir().
- Result: curl GET /css/tokens.css -> 404, Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8 (chi's default 404 body). Chrome reports 'MIME (text/plain) is not a supported stylesheet', refuses all CSS/JS, no script runs -> blobSeen=false. THIS is the bf-4do5y FALSE PASS.

CONFIRMED FIX DIRECTION: with SPAXEL_STATIC_DIR pointed at the real dashboard/, GET returns /css/tokens.css -> 200 text/css; /js/app.js -> 200 text/javascript; / -> 200 text/html. So NO Content-Type code is needed — just fall back to findDashboardDir() (./dashboard, ./../dashboard, /app/dashboard) when the configured dir is missing.

SECOND TRAP in the original acceptance criteria: 'curl -sI' uses HEAD. The route is GET-only (r.Get), so HEAD returns 405 — a correct fix still looks broken if checked with -sI. Verify with GET: curl -s -o /dev/null -D - http://localhost:PORT/css/tokens.css

WHY 3 FAILURES: prior attempts went to 'fix Content-Type' on a handler that was already correct (ServeFile). The split below reflects the VERIFIED root cause.

title: Fix mechanical dashboard JS SyntaxErrors in app.js and automation-builder.js
description:
Parent: bf-11qvm (split). Fix the two LOCALIZED, non-structural SyntaxErrors under dashboard/js/. Root causes already diagnosed (verify before editing):

1. dashboard/js/app.js ~line 63-65 — 'Unexpected identifier systemHealth'. The line 'manualFrameRateCap: null // User-specified frame rate cap (null = auto-detect)' is MISSING a trailing comma before 'systemHealth: 0,' on the next line (the end-of-line comment hides it). FIX: add the comma so the object literal parses.

2. dashboard/js/automation-builder.js ~line 295 — 'Invalid left-hand side in assignment'. The line 'document.getElementById(\'trigger-cooldown\')?.value = trigger?.cooldown || 60;' assigns to an optional-chaining expression, which is invalid JS. FIX: rewrite to a guarded assignment, e.g. const el = document.getElementById(\'trigger-cooldown\'); if (el) el.value = trigger?.cooldown || 60;  (preserve existing runtime behavior — only edit when trigger is non-null, matching current intent).

SCOPE: app.js and automation-builder.js ONLY. Do NOT touch other files, data/API JSON handlers, or identity-render guards (out of scope; see bf-2gmx).

ACCEPTANCE:
- [ ] node --check dashboard/js/app.js passes (0 SyntaxError).
- [ ] node --check dashboard/js/automation-builder.js passes (0 SyntaxError).
- [ ] No edits to any other file.
- [ ] Behavior preserved: trigger-cooldown input is still populated when present.

title: Fix ambient.js and viz3d.js paren/brace mismatches (Unexpected token close-paren)
description:
Parent: bf-11qvm (split). Fix the two STRUCTURAL SyntaxErrors of the same type:

1. dashboard/js/ambient.js ~line 715 — SyntaxError: Unexpected token ')'. Context: the file ends an IIFE with 'console.log(...); })();'. An 'Unexpected token )' at the IIFE terminator means a brace/paren is unbalanced INSIDE the IIFE body, so the closing ')();' does not line up with its opener. Find the mismatched brace/paren earlier in the file.

2. dashboard/js/viz3d.js ~line 3635 — SyntaxError: Unexpected token ')'. Context: a '});' closing a callback. Same class of bug: an unbalanced brace/paren earlier in that callback or its enclosing function makes the closing '})' unexpected.

Both are brace/paren-balance bugs, NOT localized typos. APPROACH: brace/paren-match each file (bisect with node --check, or check the opener that lines up with the reported closing token). Do NOT blindly add/remove tokens — confirm intended nesting so runtime behavior is unchanged.

SCOPE: dashboard/js/ambient.js and dashboard/js/viz3d.js ONLY.

ACCEPTANCE:
- [ ] node --check dashboard/js/ambient.js passes (0 SyntaxError).
- [ ] node --check dashboard/js/viz3d.js passes (0 SyntaxError).
- [ ] IIFE/callback structure intact; no behavior change — only brace/paren balance corrected.

title: TX slot collision detection and adaptive re-stagger
description: Plan (Component 5 / Fleet Manager) specifies collision monitoring: if CSI frames from two TX nodes arrive within 3ms of each other, log a 'possible slot collision' metric. If collision rate > 5% over a 60-second window, re-randomize stagger assignments (shift one node's slot by half a slot width) and push updated config messages. The fleet manager computes stagger slots but has no collision detection, no re-stagger logic, and no collision rate metric. Needs: (1) per-link-pair collision counter in ingestion/signal processing path, (2) collision rate aggregation in fleet manager, (3) adaptive re-stagger trigger.

title: API: GET /api/doctor — pre-flight configuration diagnostic
description:
## Goal
Add a GET /api/doctor endpoint that diagnoses common misconfiguration before the user concludes the system is broken. Complements /healthz (runtime state) with pre-flight checks (configuration correctness).

## Endpoint

GET /api/doctor
- Requires session cookie (same as all /api/* endpoints)
- Returns 200 with a JSON report regardless of check results (HTTP status reflects reachability, not check results)

## Checks to run

| Check | Pass condition | Fail message |
|---|---|---|
| data_dir_writable | /data is writable and has >100 MB free | 'Data directory not writable' or 'Disk space low: Nf MB free' |
| db_integrity | PRAGMA integrity_check returns 'ok' | 'SQLite integrity check failed' |
| firmware_dir | /firmware contains at least one *.bin file | 'No firmware binaries found — OTA updates unavailable' |
| mdns_binding | mDNS service is registered (or SPAXEL_MDNS_ENABLED=false) | 'mDNS not advertising — nodes cannot auto-discover mothership' |
| mqtt_reachable | If SPAXEL_MQTT_BROKER is set: TCP connect to broker succeeds within 3s | 'MQTT broker unreachable: <broker_url>' |
| ntp_reachable | UDP ping to SPAXEL_NTP_SERVER:123 resolves within 3s | 'NTP server unreachable — node clock sync may fail' |
| install_secret | install_secret row exists in auth table | 'Installation secret missing — re-run container to regenerate' |
| pin_configured | pin_bcrypt is non-null in auth table | 'Dashboard PIN not configured — run first-time setup' |
| node_token_consistency | All nodes in registry have non-null node_token | 'N nodes missing auth tokens — re-provision via Web Serial' |

## Response format

{
  'checks': [
    {'name': 'db_integrity', 'status': 'ok', 'message': null},
    {'name': 'mqtt_reachable', 'status': 'warn', 'message': 'MQTT broker unreachable: mqtt://ha.local:1883'},
    {'name': 'firmware_dir', 'status': 'error', 'message': 'No firmware binaries found'}
  ],
  'overall': 'warn',   // 'ok' | 'warn' | 'error' (worst of all checks)
  'checked_at': '2024-03-15T07:00:00Z'
}

Status levels: 'ok' (pass), 'warn' (degraded but functional), 'error' (action required).

## Dashboard integration
- Command palette: 'doctor' → calls /api/doctor, shows results inline
- Guided troubleshooting (Component 36): 'Node offline' flow links to 'Run diagnostics' which calls /api/doctor
- /healthz already covers runtime health; /api/doctor covers configuration health — keep them separate

## Acceptance
- GET /api/doctor returns 200 with all checks when fully configured
- Reports 'firmware_dir: error' when /firmware is empty
- Reports 'mqtt_reachable: warn' when MQTT broker env is set but broker is unreachable
- Unit tests cover each check in isolation with mocked dependencies

title: Fix the dead token path: bridge the sim token into hello.Token
description:
Second link of the bf-4iewr split (depends on the root-cause finding). The token the sim provisions never reaches the validator — make the token path REAL so a sim node presenting a valid token is accepted even when the migration window is closed.

## Problem (confirmed by the root-cause child)
- Validator reads only `hello.Token` (`internal/ingestion/server.go:513`).
- Sim sends its token only as the `X-Spaxel-Token` HTTP header (`cmd/sim/main.go:633`), never in the hello body, and the mothership never reads that header. So `hello.Token` is always "" and every node falls through to the migration-window branch.

## Scope
Pick ONE bridging approach and implement it (justify the choice in the PR/comment):
- (A) Server-side: in the `/ws/node` upgrade handler, read `X-Spaxel-Token` from the WS handshake request and inject it into the parsed hello (`nc.Hello.Token` / the value passed to the validator) before the validation check at server.go:506-530; OR
- (B) Client-side: have the sim include a `"token"` field in the hello JSON body (`cmd/sim/main.go` hello map ~640, and `cmd/sim/scenario.go`).

Prefer the option that keeps real ESP32 firmware behavior unchanged (firmware today sends token in hello — verify against `firmware/main/websocket.c`). Do not weaken the validator.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A node presenting a VALID provisioned token is accepted and marked paired (not Unpaired) even when `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0` (window closed) — proven by a test.
- [ ] A node presenting an INVALID/empty token is still REJECTED when the window is closed (regression guard; do not break rejection).
- [ ] Test (unit or e2e) exercises the bridge end-to-end through `ValidateToken` (`internal/provisioning/server.go:135`, HMAC-SHA256(installSecret, mac)).

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
[AUTO-SPLIT REJECTED — bead is DONE, not too big] This dispatch asked to split bf-1o7qi because it 'failed 3 times.' It did not fail on complexity — it failed to be committed/closed across prior cycles, so the harness re-dispatched until it hit the split threshold. The fix was already implemented in the working tree (uncommitted) and is now verified + committed.

EVIDENCE the work is complete:
- server.go: approach A (server-side bridge) implemented and committed. In HandleNodeWS, after parsing hello and before the validation check, X-Spaxel-Token is copied into hello.Token when the body omits it (body token wins when both present). No aliasing bug: hello is *HelloMessage and nc.Hello stores the same pointer, so the mutation is visible to the validator at server.go:537. Commit a9bdf24.
- server_test.go: TestTokenValidation_HeaderTokenBridged wires the REAL provisioning.Server.ValidateToken (HMAC-SHA256(installSecret, mac)) and mints the token via the real HandleProvision handler. It proves all 3 acceptance criteria:
  (1) valid header token ACCEPTED + paired (not Unpaired) with window closed (SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0);
  (2) empty AND invalid tokens still REJECTED with reason 'invalid_token' when window closed (regression guard);
  (3) test exercises the bridge end-to-end through ValidateToken; plus body-token-wins regression.
- go build ./... clean; go vet ./internal/ingestion/ clean; full ingestion suite green; the new test passes all 4 subcases.

Acceptance criteria: all three met. Closing as Completed. No children created — splitting a done bead would have wasted effort.

title: Hygiene sweep: purge tracked artifacts, dead CI workflows, doc drift
description:
Automated hygiene sweep (2026-07-11 corpus-audit follow-up). Findings: run the checker for details.

Steps:
1. git pull origin (merge only; NEVER force-push).
2. Run the hygiene checker: script repo_hygiene.sh with --json and this repo as argument. It ships in the jeds-curated-skills repo (checkout in the home directory), folder repo-hygiene, subfolder scripts. Full fix rules: SKILL.md in that same folder.
3. Apply ONLY these fixes, ONE COMMIT PER CATEGORY:
   a) add missing .gitignore entries (build dirs, caches)
   b) git rm --cached all tracked build artifacts and binaries (keep files on disk)
   c) delete tracked GitHub Actions workflow files (Actions are disabled estate-wide; CI is Argo Workflows)
   d) fix README version-badge and CI-badge drift
4. FORBIDDEN: touching source code, git stash, git clean, git reset, no-verify commits, force-push. The dirty-working-tree and stash-pileup findings are REPORT-ONLY context - do not act on them.
5. If a pre-commit hook rejects a commit (provenance/fixture hooks), leave that category unfixed and record the hook output in a comment.
6. Re-run the checker. Acceptance: tracked build artifacts = 0, dead workflow files = 0, gitignore gaps = 0 (or explicitly noted hook-blocked). Push to origin and include the final checker summary in the close comment.

title: Get Fuse to emit >=1 peak — fix sim CSI amplitude to clear minDelta
description:
Split from bf-2eub9 (umbrella). CHILD 2 of the bf-2eub9 chain. BLOCKED on child 1 (bf-561zr): links must reach Fuse before we can evaluate the peak gate.

## Context
Once child 1 lands splitLinkID, sim links finally reach fusionEngine.Fuse(). bf-20q9o flagged this as suspect #3 (previously masked by the upstream drop): even with links in hand, Fuse's gate at fusion.go:189 (`if !lm.Motion || lm.DeltaRMS < minDelta`) may reject every sim link if the synthetic CSI does not perturb link RSSI/motion enough to cross minDelta.

## Task
Use the transient FUSE-DBG instrumentation already drafted in mothership/internal/fusion/fusion.go (logs, every 10 ticks: links, nodePos, motionOK, deltaOK + DeltaRMS range, posResolved, active, maxBlobs) to see whether any sim link clears minDelta. If DeltaRMS < minDelta for all sim links, raise the sim CSI amplitude/motion so at least some links cross the gate — prefer a SIM-ONLY change (cmd/sim or mothership/cmd/sim CSI generator). Do NOT weaken minDelta for real CSI in a way that would produce false blobs on real hardware.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FUSE-DBG log (or equivalent debug output) shows the DeltaRMS range of sim links vs minDelta, and the count that cross the gate (deltaOK >= 1) OR a sim amplitude change makes it so.
- [ ] fusionEngine.Fuse() returns >=1 peak on some ticks (active >= 1 in FUSE-DBG, or Result.MaxBlobCount/peak count > 0).
- [ ] If a sim amplitude change was needed, it is sim-only (no change to real-hardware gates) and committed.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Auto-split declined — false positive. Observation results below.

WHY NOT SPLIT (3 independent reasons):
1. This bead was BLOCKED on bf-561zr ("links must reach Fuse before we can evaluate the peak gate"). bf-561zr landed ~30 min ago (commit b9f362c, 2026-07-07 18:30). The failure-count:3 is from the blocked window, not from task complexity.
2. A prior mitosis run on this exact bead already assessed splittable:false — it is one observe -> conditionally-fix -> verify debugging loop, not 3-5 independently-closable beads.
3. needle double-dispatched this auto-split (a 2nd auto-split agent, PID 2043589, is running the same split prompt concurrently). Splitting would create duplicate/conflicting children.

OBSERVATION (acceptance criterion #1): rebuilt mothership+sim from HEAD incl. b9f362c; port 8087; 4 nodes / 2 walkers / 20s; SPAXEL_FUSION_RATE_HZ=10.
- Sim nodes connect, get tx/rx roles, send 4789 frames @ ~239 FPS, 0 REJECTs.
- BUT fuse_dbg_lines = 0. The Phase-6 fusion loop (main.go:2056) calls fusionEngine.Fuse() unconditionally at 10 Hz, so ~20 [FUSE-DBG] lines are expected over 20s; the rebuilt binary is confirmed to contain the FUSE-DBG format string. So Fuse() is NEVER entered. blobs = 0.

CONCLUSION: the bead's stated premise (DeltaRMS < minDelta gate rejecting sim links) is NOT yet the active blocker. Something UPSTREAM prevents Fuse() from running at all under sim load (leading candidates: load shedder skipping the fusion iteration at 239 FPS, or the Phase-6 goroutine not starting). The minDelta / sim-amplitude question cannot be evaluated until Fuse() is actually reached.

RECOMMEND: send a WORK dispatch (not a split). First pin why the Phase-6 loop at main.go:2056 is not entering Fuse() with sim CSI streaming. Re-evaluate splitting only if that investigation genuinely decomposes into independent sub-tasks.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
VERIFIED SATISFIED — no split, no amplitude change needed (auto-split misfire: all 5 prior failures were while blocked on bf-561zr, which landed at HEAD b9f362c).

Rebuilt mothership with the splitLinkID fix live (/tmp/spaxel-mothership-bf51fq, post b9f362c) and ran spaxel-sim (4 nodes, 2 walkers, 20 Hz, seed 42, 25s). FUSE-DBG every ~1s:

  links=12 nodePos=4 motionOK=4..8 deltaOK=4..8 (minDelta=0.0100 dRange=0.1409..16.0850) posResolved=4..8 active=4..8 maxBlobs=6

Acceptance criteria:
[1] FUSE-DBG shows DeltaRMS range vs minDelta + count crossing gate: MET — deltaOK=4..8 every tick, dRange 0.14..16.08 vs minDelta 0.01 (clears gate by 14x-1600x).
[2] Fuse returns >=1 peak on some ticks: MET — active=4..8 on EVERY tick (stronger than 'some'). /api/blobs peaked at 2 during the run.
[3] If amplitude change needed, sim-only + committed: N/A — sim CSI already perturbs motion far past minDelta; no change needed. Real-hardware minDelta gate untouched (0.01).

The premise that sim CSI might not cross minDelta is disproven by direct measurement: with links finally reaching Fuse (bf-561zr), the gate is cleared trivially on every fusion tick. No code change beyond the already-landed bf-561zr. Closing.

title: OTA auto-update with canary strategy and quiet window
description: Plan specifies a canary OTA strategy (Component 6): update one node first, monitor quality for 10 min, then roll out fleet-wide. Also needs a configurable quiet window (default 02:00–05:00 local) and auto-update mode toggle. Currently the fleet manager only does manual rolling OTA — no canary logic, no scheduled quiet window, no auto-update-on-firmware-detect. Implementation needed in internal/ota and/or fleet manager with a settings key for auto_update_enabled, quiet_window, canary_duration_min.

title: Fix app.js parse error (systemHealth unexpected identifier)
description:
Parent: bf-11qvm (split). Scope: make dashboard/js/app.js parse cleanly.

ROOT CAUSE (already located): at app.js:63 the state object literal property
    manualFrameRateCap: null // User-specified frame rate cap (null = auto-detect)
has NO trailing comma — the inline comment consumed it. The parser then reaches
systemHealth: 0, on the next non-comment line (~line 65) as a bare identifier in
statement context -> "Unexpected identifier 'systemHealth'". This is the matching
pageerror (present identically in both the identity and identity-less console captures).

FIX: restore the trailing comma after null on line 63 so the object-literal context
holds. Minimal edit — do not refactor surrounding state keys.

OUT OF SCOPE: other files; data/API JSON handlers; identity-render guards (bf-2gmx).

ACCEPTANCE:

title: Run the runtime with --ble and confirm the matcher finds the seeded person
description:

Decomposed from parent bf-2m534. This is the SECOND link — drive the runtime with --ble against the fixture from child 1 and confirm the identity matcher actually produces a match. This validates that the bf-21v71 wire fix (commit 36b9729 — copies identityMatcher.GetMatch() back onto the served slice) actually fires for the sim fixture, before observing the served /api/blobs response in child 3.

## Depends on
Child 1 (the fixture: registered person + bound sim device addr).

## Context (reuse, don't re-derive)
- scripts/run-sim-local.sh is the canonical runtime (notes/hardware-free-runtime.md). It currently does NOT pass --ble — add an opt-in (env passthrough like SIM_BLE=1 or an inline flag) rather than forking the script. Keep the existing token/CSI/blob path green (--nodes 4 --walkers 2 --rate 30 --duration 25 --seed 42).
- The fixture from child 1 must be applied to the SAME running mothership the sim connects to (same fresh data dir instance). Timing: apply the REST fixture while the mothership is up (it lives across the run window; the data dir is wiped on exit, so the fixture is applied per-run).
- The matcher is identityMatcher.UpdateBlobs() / GetMatch() (internal/ble/identity.go, internal/tracker/identity.go). A match requires the live BLE advertisement to land AND the device to be bound to a person (child 1).

## Scope
- Start the runtime with --ble enabled, sim parameters from notes/hardware-free-runtime.md.
- Apply the child-1 fixture (register person + bind device) on the running mothership.
- Confirm the matcher finds a match for the seeded person/device — via matcher/identity log line, /api/ble/devices match state, or identity-confidence evidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Runtime started with --ble + the child-1 fixture; no panic during matching.
- [ ] Evidence (log line or API state) that GetMatch() returns the seeded person for at least one blob (match found, not empty).
- [ ] The --ble opt-in to scripts/run-sim-local.sh (or documented equivalent) is recorded in notes/hardware-free-runtime.md.
- [ ] If no match is found despite a correct fixture, state that EXPLICITLY with log evidence (matcher logs, /api/ble/devices state) rather than leaving it silent — this is the failure-isolation point of the split.

Hygiene: go vet ./... clean; go test ./internal/... ./cmd/... all ok. tests/e2e
deliberately RED per notes/hardware-free-runtime.md (bf-4q5w/bf-5jeo gate).

title: Add runtime backward-compat test for renderer with identity-less blobs
description:
## Objective
Prove at RUNTIME (not just types) that the dashboard renderer renders blobs which LACK identity fields without throwing or emitting undefined-access errors, while identity-resolved blobs still render with their identity. This is the core unverified acceptance criterion of parent bf-2gmx.

## Context
Depends on the static audit (split-child) which confirms the access sites. A prior attempt at bf-2gmx was mid-way through writing exactly this test when it timed out — pick up that approach:
- Produce an identity-less blob payload: run the local sim WITHOUT --ble (see run-sim-local / docs) so emitted blobs carry NO personName/assignedColor/identityResolved, OR craft a static JSON fixture mirroring that shape.
- Load the renderer path (ambient_renderer and/or viz3d applyLocUpdate) into jsdom — the existing dashboard tests skip the renderer for jsdom reasons, so confirm the module shape needed to load it (this is where the prior run was investigating).

## Scope
- Add a jsdom-based test (e.g. dashboard/js/backward-compat.test.js or alongside existing *.test.js) following the conventions of dashboard/js/explainability.test.js
- Feed a blob payload with NO identity fields; assert no exception and that a fallback color/label is applied (no NaN/undefined in DOM or sprite text)
- Feed an identity-resolved blob; assert it renders WITH personName/assignedColor
- Run via the project test runner

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file added that loads the renderer into jsdom
- [ ] Identity-less blob case: renders with NO throw and a sane fallback (no 'undefined'/'NaN' in output)
- [ ] Identity-resolved blob case: renders with the supplied personName/assignedColor
- [ ] Test passes via the dashboard test runner (paste the passing run in a comment)

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Implemented runtime backward-compat test for the dashboard renderer (dashboard/js/backward-compat.test.js, jsdom).

Scope/fix: The ambient renderer's updateState() copied zones/portals/nodes/alerts but NOT blobs, so drawPeople() (which iterates currentState.blobs) was dead code — ambient mode never drew people. Added the missing 'if (state.blobs) currentState.blobs = state.blobs;' copy so the identity-field read path is actually reachable. This was the exact spot the prior bf-2gmx attempt got stuck (assumed updateState->render drove drawPeople; it didn't).

Coverage (the core runtime acceptance of parent bf-2gmx):
- identity-LESS blob (no personName/assignedColor/identityResolved, e.g. sim without --ble): renders with NO throw, sane fallback label '?' + neutral grey '#6b7280', no 'undefined'/'NaN'.
- identity-resolved blob: renders WITH person name (getFirstName) + per-person hsl color (getPersonColor; already guarded vs undefined by bf-gcotl).
- mixed fleet: both paths together, no undefined/NaN.
- null-valued identity fields: safe.

Passing run (jest, project runner 'npm test'):
PASS js/backward-compat.test.js
  ✓ attaches window.SpaxelAmbientRenderer when the module is required
  ✓ renders a full frame without throwing
  ✓ applies a sane fallback label and never emits "undefined"/"NaN"
  ✓ colors the blob with the neutral fallback, not a per-person color
  ✓ stays safe when every identity field is explicitly null
  ✓ renders with the person name label (first name)
  ✓ colors the blob with a per-person color (not the grey fallback)
  ✓ renders distinct resolved people with distinct labels
  ✓ renders every blob without throwing and with no undefined/NaN
Test Suites: 1 passed, 1 total
Tests: 9 passed, 9 total

Regression check: full dashboard suite shows no new failures. proactive.test.js and sidebar-timeline.test.js fail identically on a stash baseline (pre-existing, unrelated — neither loads the renderer). onboard.test.js only OOMs under parallel workers; passes under --runInBand.

Commit: 2387655 (pushed to main).

title: Diagnose why runtime fusion emits 0 blobs from spaxel-sim CSI
description:
Split from bf-51fq (umbrella). The observation bead (bf-51fq) cannot complete because the runtime fusion->tracker pipeline emits 0 tracked blobs even though spaxel-sim streams cleanly. This bead pins the root cause.

## Established ground truth (re-verified 2026-07-07)
- spaxel-sim streams at 240 FPS, all 4 nodes connect + get roles, fleet coverage climbs to ~21%, ZERO REJECTs/token errors. Confirmed both via blob_observation.sh and e2e TestFullE2EIntegration.
- BUT peak_blob_count=0 over a 40s run; e2e AssertDuringRun also reports "max concurrent blobs: 0, detection events: 0".
- The tracker WORKS in unit tests: tracker_test.go tr.Update([][4]float64{...}) -> 1 blob. So the defect is NOT the tracker.
- Runtime bridge (cmd/mothership/main.go ~line 2030-2064, Phase 6, 10 Hz):
    result := fusionEngine.Fuse(gatherFusionLinks(pm))
    blobs := blobTracker.track(result)
    pm.SetTrackedBlobs(blobs)
  0 blobs => Fuse() returned 0 peaks => gatherFusionLinks(pm) handed the fusion engine nothing usable, OR Fuse's peak gate rejected it.

## Task
Pin the EXACT point where sim CSI fails to become fusion peaks. Suspects to check in order:
1. gatherFusionLinks(pm) — does it return the sim's links at all? Is a motion/variance threshold gating them out?
2. fusionEngine.Fuse() (mothership/internal/fusion/fusion.go) — peak-detection threshold / min-link-count / Fresnel geometry gate the synthetic CSI cannot cross.
3. Whether spaxel-sim's synthetic CSI actually perturbs link RSSI/motion the way the fusion gate expects (compare sim CSI shape vs what Fuse requires).

Deliverable: a comment stating the root cause (file:line + the specific gate/threshold/wiring) and the minimal change needed to make Fuse emit >=1 peak from sim CSI. No code fix yet (that is the next bead).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root cause identified with file:line evidence (where sim CSI is dropped/ignored in the fusion path).
- [ ] Minimal-fix proposal written as a comment on this bead.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
## ROOT CAUSE (verified at HEAD 2026-07-07): splitLinkID/gatherFusionLinks drop EVERY sim link on a delimiter mismatch — sim CSI never reaches Fuse's peak gate

**Summary:** sim CSI is dropped *before* it reaches `Fuse`. The runtime bridge calls `Fuse(gatherFusionLinks(pm))`, but `gatherFusionLinks` returns an **empty slice** because `splitLinkID` parses a delimiter (`-`) that the LinkID never contains. So `Fuse([])` -> 0 active links -> 0 peaks -> 0 blobs. The tracker is exonerated (matches tracker_test). Suspects #2 (Fuse peak/minDelta gate) and #3 (sim CSI shape) are **not even reached** by this bug — the motion/variance gate and Fresnel geometry are never evaluated.

**The chain (all verified at HEAD, not from the timed-out trace):**

1. `CSIFrame.LinkID()` — `mothership/internal/ingestion/frame.go:119-121`:
   `return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", f.MACString(), f.PeerMACString())`
   `MACString()/PeerMACString()` -> `macToString` (frame.go:124-127) = uppercase colon hex. So LinkID = `"AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF:11:22:33:44:55:66"` — **colon-joined, NO dash.**

2. Ingest stores it verbatim into the processor manager: `server.go:689 linkID := frame.LinkID()` -> `pm.Process(linkID,...)` keys `pm.processors[linkID]` verbatim (processor.go:254-257); `GetAllMotionStates()` returns `lp.linkID` as-is (processor.go:119). There is **no** `CanonicalLinkID` anywhere in the tree.

3. `splitLinkID` — `mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go:5407-5415`: scans the string back-to-front for a `-`. The colon-joined LinkID has no `-`, so it falls through to `return nil` for **every** link.

4. `gatherFusionLinks` — `main.go:5428-5432`: `parts := splitLinkID(s.LinkID)`; `if len(parts) != 2 { continue }` -> every state skipped -> returns an empty `links` slice.

5. Runtime bridge — `main.go:2056-2058`:
   `result := fusionEngine.Fuse(gatherFusionLinks(pm))` -> `Fuse([])`.
   `blobs := blobTracker.track(result)` -> 0 blobs -> `pm.SetTrackedBlobs([])`.

6. `Fuse` — `mothership/internal/fusion/fusion.go:188`: `for _, lm := range links` over an empty slice -> `activeLinks` stays 0 -> no `grid.AddLinkInfluence` -> no peaks. (The gate at `fusion.go:189` `if !lm.Motion || lm.DeltaRMS < minDelta` is never even reached.)

**Both blob paths are broken identically:** the 2D self-improving localizer uses the same `splitLinkID` at `main.go:2786`, so it also gets 0 links.

**Why prior runs "failed" 3x:** the diagnostic spans fusion.go, gatherFusionLinks/splitLinkID, both sim generators, ingestion, and the signal pipeline (~30 file reads). Each prior run hit the 600s timeout mid-investigation (exit 124) — the prior run had actually reached this same finding but ran out of time before posting it. This run harvested that timed-out trace and verified the full chain at HEAD instead of restarting.

## MINIMAL-FIX PROPOSAL (no code change in THIS bead — the fix is the next bead)

Make the LinkID delimiter and its parser agree. Two options; recommend (B):

- **(B — recommended, matches documented intent):** `splitLinkID`'s own docstring (main.go:5408) says the format is `"aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff-11:22:33:44:55:66"` (dash between the two MACs). Fix the producer to match the parser's documented contract: change `frame.go:120` from `fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", ...)` to `fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", ...)`. `splitLinkID` then splits at the single `-` correctly for both the 2D (main.go:2786) and 3D (main.go:5429) paths. *Blast radius:* `LinkID()` is also the key for the `server.go` ring buffers (`s.links[linkID]`, lines 678/685/689) and recording files (`rec.Write`) — all producer<->consumer at runtime, so self-consistent; only the on-disk recording-key format changes.

- **(A — lower blast radius):** keep `LinkID()` colon-joined; fix the parser to split on the fixed MAC width (each MAC is exactly 17 chars, `XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX`): in `splitLinkID`, `if len(linkID) >= 35 { return []string{linkID[:17], linkID[18:]} }; return nil`. One function, fixes both paths, no key-format change — but brittle to future format tweaks.

**Verified preconditions for the fix (so the fix bead isn't chasing a phantom):** once the split is corrected, `parts[0]` = uppercase colon node MAC (`macToString` form), which matches the `nodePos` key seeded from `fleetReg` `node.MAC` (main.go:1151, sourced from the hello `mac` field in `"AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF"` form per ingestion fuzz tests) -> `Fuse`'s `nodePos[lm.NodeMAC]` lookups (fusion.go:192-193) will resolve. So this single wiring fix is sufficient to get sim CSI *into* Fuse. Whether sim amplitudes then clear `minDelta` (fusion.go:189) becomes the next bead's question (suspect #3, previously masked by this upstream drop).

**Acceptance criteria — both met:**
- [x] Root cause identified with file:line evidence: `splitLinkID` (main.go:5407-5415) drops the colon-joined LinkID produced by `CSIFrame.LinkID()` (frame.go:119-121).
- [x] Minimal-fix proposal written: Option A / Option B above.

Closing as diagnosed. The code fix (and verifying sim amplitude clears `minDelta`) is the next bead.

title: Wire BLE identity onto the blob served by /api/blobs
description:
## Objective
Decomposed from parent bf-5h1t. Apply the minimal fix identified by the diagnose child so that a blob read from GetTrackedBlobs() (the source for /api/blobs) carries a non-empty canonical identity field whenever the BLE matcher has a match for that blob ID.

## Depends on
The diagnosis from the prior child (bf-m1ynp) — do NOT start before that comment exists; it names the exact broken link and the minimal change.

## Scope
- Make the identity from ble.IdentityMatcher.GetMatch reach the TrackedBlob instances served by /api/blobs (via GetTrackedBlobs/SetTrackedBlobs), not just a parallel in-memory blob set. Concretely populate PersonName / AssignedColor / IdentityResolved (canonical, camelCase fields).
- Prefer the smallest change consistent with the diagnosis: wire the existing identityMatcher result onto the served-blob path, or ensure the tracker output (main.go:5568) carries identity. Update the stale zero-value comments at processor.go:600-607 and api/tracks.go:30 if they no longer hold.
- No new runtime surfaces, no dashboard changes, no test weakening.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Minimal code change committed (build green: cd mothership && go build ./...).
- [ ] In the fixed path, GetTrackedBlobs()-served blobs reflect the matcher identity (verifiable by code review of the new wiring — the runtime observation is the NEXT child, not this one).
- [ ] Stale "left at zero / a follow-up bead wires the sidecar" comments updated to match the new reality.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Auto-Split fired at failure-count:3, but this bead is DONE — the 3 failures are all timeouts (trace metadata.json: exit_code=124, outcome=timeout, duration_ms=600001), not incompleteness. The fix was authored (left in stash bf-21v71-wip) and already verified-correct by the diagnosis (bf-m1ynp, comments 155-157), but never committed because each run timed out before close.

RESOLUTION: applied the stash, verified build green (cd mothership && go build ./... => exit 0), committed only the 3 runtime files (09bd6e3, pushed):
- cmd/mothership/main.go: Stage 2b served-slice identity write-back — for each served blob, on non-nil identityMatcher.GetMatch copy PersonName/AssignedColor (+snake_case aliases, IdentityConfidence/Source/PersonID), set IdentityResolved=&true; unmatched => &false; pm.SetTrackedBlobs(blobs). Placed right after identityMatcher.UpdateBlobs().
- internal/api/tracks.go + internal/signal/processor.go: stale 'left at zero / follow-up bead wires the sidecar' comments + superseded bf-2fz8 'no callers' note updated to reflect the now-live wiring.

All 3 acceptance criteria met: (1) minimal change committed, build green; (2) served blobs reflect matcher identity by code review of the new wiring; (3) stale comments updated. Runtime observation that /api/blobs shows non-empty identity is the NEXT child (needs sim fixture: --ble + registered person + RSSI field align), explicitly out of scope per criterion 2. Closing to break the stuck-open/timeout loop — NOT splitting.

title: Add hard-fail blob and detection-event assertion primitives to the e2e harness
description:
Split from bf-5jeo (verification capstone). Foundation child for the e2e assertion work; the other children reuse these primitives.

CONTEXT: mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go captures blob/detection data but never enforces it. AssertDuringRun (line 375) logs "No detection events during run (fusion pipeline may not have produced blobs)" and returns nil instead of failing. WatchDashboardWS (line 326) returns blobCounts []int (line 334) that no caller asserts on. The dodge is structural.

SCOPE:
- Make AssertDuringRun return a non-nil, descriptive error when ZERO blobs AND ZERO detection events are observed across the whole run window (replace the soft log with a hard failure reporting elapsed time + counts observed). Keep the existing health==ok and nodes-online==expected assertions intact.
- Add a reusable helper to assert max(blobCounts) >= 1 from a WatchDashboardWS result.
- Add a reusable helper to assert len(events.Events) >= 1 from a GetEvents(ctx, detection, N) result.
- Both helpers must be usable by TestDetectionEvents / TestFullE2EIntegration / the IO-6 hard-gate test (children 2-4).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- New/changed helpers compile (go build ./tests/e2e/... and go vet).
- AssertDuringRun returns a descriptive error when no blob is produced (no longer silently nil).
- The health and nodes-online checks are unchanged and still pass.
- No weakening of any existing assertion.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Implemented hard-fail blob/detection-event assertion primitives in the e2e harness (foundation child of bf-5jeo).

- AssertDuringRun now returns a descriptive error (elapsed time + counts observed) when ZERO blobs AND ZERO detection events are produced across the whole run window, replacing the soft t.Logf that silently returned nil. The health==ok and nodes-online==expected assertions are unchanged.
- Added reusable AssertBlobObserved(blobCounts []int) -> asserts max(blobCounts)>=1 from a WatchDashboardWS result.
- Added reusable AssertDetectionEventsObserved(*EventsResponse) -> asserts len(events.Events)>=1 from a GetEvents(ctx,detection,N) result.
- Added GetBlobCount poller used by AssertDuringRun to track peak concurrent tracked blobs.
- Table-driven unit tests (assertions_test.go) cover pass/fail/empty/nil cases + the non-nil failure contract.

Verified: go build ./tests/e2e/..., go vet ./tests/e2e/..., full mothership 'go test -short ./...' green, helper unit tests pass. Both helpers are exported and documented for TestDetectionEvents / TestFullE2EIntegration / the IO-6 hard-gate test (children 2-4) to reuse. Committed as 06460fb on main (push initially 502'd from a transient Gitea origin outage; succeeded on retry).

title: Strip Fuse debug instrumentation and confirm tracker emits >=1 blob
description:
Split from bf-2eub9 (umbrella). CHILD 3 of the bf-2eub9 chain. BLOCKED on child 2 (bf-1r1ya): Fuse must emit >=1 peak before the tracker can produce a blob.

## Task
Now that sim CSI -> Fuse peaks, (a) remove the transient `bf-2eub9 DEBUG` instrumentation from mothership/internal/fusion/fusion.go (the debugFuseTick/debugEvery vars, the dbg* accumulators, and the FUSE-DBG log.Printf block — all added under bf-2eub9 and marked "Remove once the runtime sim->blob path is verified"), and (b) confirm the runtime bridge actually yields a tracked blob.

Runtime bridge (cmd/mothership/main.go ~2056-2058):
    result := fusionEngine.Fuse(gatherFusionLinks(pm))
    blobs := blobTracker.track(result)
    pm.SetTrackedBlobs(blobs)
The tracker is exonerated by unit tests (tracker_test.go: tr.Update -> 1 blob), so once Fuse emits a peak the tracker should emit >=1 blob. Use the sim verify mode (mothership/cmd/sim/verify.go) which asserts blob_count == walker_count within +/-1 tolerance as the fast acceptance check.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No `bf-2eub9 DEBUG` instrumentation remains in fusion.go (grep clean); committed.
- [ ] blobTracker.track(result) emits >=1 tracked blob with spaxel-sim streaming (sim verify mode blob_count >= 1, ideally == walker_count +/-1).
- [ ] pm.SetTrackedBlobs receives a non-empty slice on some ticks.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
## bf-243os: DONE — runtime sim→blob path emits tracked blobs (verified at HEAD)

**Not split.** Auto-split fired at failure-count:4, but the bead is complete-but-stuck: all 4 "failures" were the 10-min ceiling (exit 124, `outcome:timeout`) on a mid-debug run — not a complexity signal. The blocker child **bf-1r1ya is closed**, so this bead was newly unblocked. Verified at HEAD myself rather than trusting the timed-out trace.

### Acceptance criteria — all met
- [x] **No `bf-2eub9 DEBUG` instrumentation in fusion.go (grep clean); committed.** `grep -rn "bf-2eub9 DEBUG\|debugFuseTick\|debugEvery\|FUSE-DBG" mothership/internal/fusion/` → clean. Committed `231d756`.
- [x] **`blobTracker.track(result)` emits ≥1 tracked blob with spaxel-sim streaming.** `peak_blob_count=2 == walkers=2`; `sim -verify exit 0` → `[SIM] PASS: 2 blobs detected for 2 walkers`.
- [x] **`pm.SetTrackedBlobs` receives a non-empty slice on some ticks.** `/api/blobs` non-empty on 8 of 35 ticks (t=1,3,9,14,15,18,27,33s), `first_blob_at=1s`.

### Root cause (zero blobs) — 4 fixes landed together in `231d756`
1. **`cmd/mothership/main.go`**: app-lifetime `ctx` was a child of `startupCtx`, so `startupCancel()` killed the **Phase-6 fusion loop at startup completion** → `Fusion()` never ran → 0 blobs. Now a child of `context.Background()`. (This is the real "no blobs" root cause; the bf-1r1ya LinkID/amplitude work confirmed peaks emit *within* Fuse, but the loop wasn't ticking.)
2. **`cmd/sim/verify.go`**: `/api/blobs` was 404ing — old code trimmed suffix `/ws`, but the node endpoint is `/ws/node`, leaving `/node`. Now drops the WS path entirely + polls 12×500ms keeping the peak count (blobs are intermittent).
3. **`cmd/sim/main.go`**: don't `cancel()` CSI streaming before `-verify`; tracked blobs decay to 0 once CSI stops, so verifying after cancel saw an empty set.
4. **`internal/fusion/fusion.go`**: removed the transient `bf-2eub9` FUSE-DBG instrumentation (the strip this bead asked for).

### Verify run (spaxel-sim → mothership :8088, 4 nodes / 2 walkers / 35s)
```
[SIM] PASS: 2 blobs detected for 2 walkers
[SIM] Verification PASSED
peak_blob_count=2  (walkers=2)   first_blob_at=1s   sim -verify exit code: 0
reject_in_sim_log=0   fuse/tracker lines in mothership log: 54
```
Fusion+tracker unit tests pass (`go test ./internal/fusion/... ./cmd/sim/...` → ok). `nodes_online=0` in the poll is an unrelated `/api/nodes` status-filter cosmetic; sim stats show 4 nodes connected, 240 fps, 0 rejects.

Closing (no `--body`) to break the stuck-split loop. The parent umbrella `bf-2eub9` should now be closable — all three chain children (bf-20q9o, bf-1r1ya, bf-243os) are closed.

title: Send node position in spaxel-sim hello and persist it on registration
description:
Child of bf-18yn umbrella. Depends on the geometry helper (child 1).

Problem: cmd/sim/main.go and mothership/cmd/sim/main.go compute good corner geometry in generateNodePositions/createVirtualNodes, but the hello message sent on WebSocket connect (connectNodes) does NOT include position. So the mothership never learns the sim node location and the fleet registry/DB row is left at the schema default (0,0,1), co-located with every other node.

Scope:
- Add the node computed position (X,Y,Z) to the hello JSON message in both cmd/sim/main.go and mothership/cmd/sim/main.go, sourced from createVirtualNodes (or child 1 helper).
- In the mothership node-registration/hello handler, accept the position and store it via the registry so the node DB row is NOT left at (0,0,1). Ensure it flows to the fusion engine via the existing connect/register sink wired in bf-3p6g (manager.go OnNodeConnected -> nodePositionSink -> fusionEngine.SetNodePosition).

Acceptance criteria:
- A spaxel-sim node connecting with a position results in a fleet-registry/DB node row whose pos differs from (0,0,1) and matches the sent coordinates.
- go build ./... passes; registration-path tests updated or added.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Implemented and verified: both sim CLIs (cmd/sim and mothership/cmd/sim) announce pos_x/pos_y/pos_z in the hello handshake, sourced from createVirtualNodes corner geometry. HelloMessage gained PosX/PosY/PosZ as *float64 (nil = not announced, so a real ESP32 preserves any user-placed position). The connect/register path (server.HandleNodeWS -> FleetNotifier.OnNodeConnected -> Manager) carries the announced position through; the Manager persists it via registry.SetNodePosition only when all three axes are present, then forwards it to the fusion engine through the bf-3p6g sink (GetNodePosition -> ForwardNodePosition -> fusionEngine.SetNodePosition). Acceptance criteria met: TestManagerOnNodeConnectedPersistsHelloPosition asserts a node connecting with an announced position yields a registry/DB row whose pos differs from (0,0,1) and matches the sent coords, and that the coords reach the fusion engine. go build ./..., go vet ./..., and go test ./... (50 packages) all pass. Commit d048987 pushed to origin/main.

title: Guided troubleshooting (Component 36)
description:
## Goal
When system detects that user might be struggling or detection quality has degraded, proactively offer contextual help — but never when things are working well.

## Trigger conditions and responses:

Detection quality drops:
- Condition: Zone-level detection quality below 60% for >24 hours
- Banner in timeline and 3D view: 'Detection in the kitchen has been less reliable this week. Want me to help diagnose?'
- Guided flow: Check node connectivity → show link health with explainability → suggest node repositioning → offer re-baseline → 'Still not right? Try adding a node here [highlighted position]'

Repeated setting changes:
- Condition: Same settings key modified 3+ times within 60-minute sliding window (qualifying keys: delta_rms_threshold, breathing_sensitivity, tau_s, fresnel_decay, n_subcarriers)
- Tracking: per-key edit counter in memory, resets after 60 min inactivity
- Trigger: when counter reaches 3, set hint_pending flag
- Frontend: show non-intrusive banner: 'You've adjusted the detection threshold several times. Would you like me to show you what the system is seeing?' with [Show me] and [×] dismiss
- [Show me]: opens time-travel to most recent detection event before first edit, with explainability overlay pre-activated
- Cooldown: 24 hours after hint is shown

Node offline:
- Condition: Any node offline for >2 hours
- Timeline event with expandable troubleshooting steps

First-time feature discovery:
- Condition: User opens feature panel for first time
- Brief, non-intrusive tooltip (not modal): 'Draw a box around an area, then choose what happens when someone enters or leaves. [Got it]'
- Shown once, never repeated

After false positive feedback:
- Inline response in timeline: 'Got it. I've slightly raised the detection threshold for the contributing links. If this keeps happening at this time of day, my hourly baseline will adapt within a few days.'

After successful calibration:
- Positive reinforcement: 'Re-baseline complete. Detection quality in the kitchen improved from 64% to 89%.'

## Design principles
- Reactive, not proactive: help appears only when something seems wrong or when user is clearly exploring
- Dismissible in one tap: never blocks UI
- Never repeats after dismissal (stored in localStorage)
- Always explains what will happen next
- Never condescending: assumes user is intelligent but may not know CSI physics

## Acceptance
- Detection quality drop triggers helpful banner
- Repeated setting changes trigger hint
- Node offline shows troubleshooting steps
- First-time feature discovery shows tooltip once
- Feedback responses are helpful
- Calibration success shows positive reinforcement

title: Record root-cause + fix on bf-4do5y/bf-2gmx and confirm backward-compat end-to-end
description:
