title: Command palette (Component 34)
description:
## Goal
Ctrl+K (Cmd+K on Mac) opens universal search and command interface. Invisible to casual users, indispensable for power users.

## Scope
Search:
- 'kitchen' → Kitchen zone, kitchen nodes, kitchen automations, recent kitchen events
- 'alice' → Alice's current location, today's timeline, sleep report, BLE devices
- 'node 3' → Node details, diagnostics, link health

Navigate time:
- 'last night 2am' → timeline jumps there
- 'yesterday kitchen' → filters timeline to kitchen events yesterday
- 'this morning' → jumps to first detection today

Execute commands:
- 'update all nodes', 're-baseline kitchen', 'add node', 'arm security', 'disarm security'
- 'dark mode'/'light mode', 'export config', 'restart node kitchen-north'

Get help:
- 'help fall detection', 'why false positive', 'troubleshoot kitchen'

Behavior:
- Fuzzy matching: 'flr pln' matches 'Floor Plan settings'
- Recently used commands appear first
- Results show keyboard shortcut hints where applicable
- Escape closes, Enter executes top result
- Works in expert mode only

## Implementation
Frontend-only component
Command registry maps keywords to actions
Search runs against: zone names, person names, node names, setting names, help topics

## Acceptance
- Ctrl+K/Cmd+K opens command palette
- Search finds zones, people, nodes, settings, help topics
- Commands execute correctly
- Time navigation jumps to correct moments
- Fuzzy matching works
- Escape closes palette
close_reason:

- Ctrl+K/Cmd+K opens universal search and command interface

- Recently used commands appear first (up to 5)
- Escape closes, Enter executes top result
- Works in expert mode only (excluded from simple/ambient modes)
- All 88 tests pass

title: Propagate runtime node position changes to the fusion engine
description:
Split from bf-4q5w (parent umbrella). Depends on the startup-seeding child.

Today PATCH /api/nodes/:mac updates only the fleet registry: internal/fleet/handler.go updateNodePosition calls h.mgr.GetRegistry().SetNodePosition (~handler.go:391) and BroadcastRegistry, but never forwards the new position to the fusion engine. Likewise node connect/register events do not push positions to the engine, so a node moved at runtime stays at its stale position in the blob engine.

Scope:
- On PATCH node position (handler.go updateNodePosition), after updating the registry, call the blob-producing fusion engine SetNodePosition(mac,x,y,z) for the engine wired in the prior child.
- On node connect/register, push the node current position to the engine as well.
- Decide where the engine reference lives so the handler can reach it (e.g. via the registry/fleet manager or a setter injected at startup), matching existing accessor patterns such as SetNodePositionAccessor in internal/diagnostics and internal/fleet/weather.go.

Acceptance criteria:
- PATCH /api/nodes/:mac and node registration both update the fusion engine position for that MAC.
- Existing fleet handler tests (internal/fleet/handler_test.go) still pass.
- A test asserts the position the engine stores for a MAC changes after a PATCH with new coordinates.
- go build ./... passes.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Implemented bf-3p6g: runtime node positions now propagate to the fusion engine. Added Manager.nodePositionSink (write-side mirror of the read-side SetNodePositionAccessor used by diagnostics/weather.go), wired to fusionEngine.SetNodePosition in main.go. Fires from both position paths: PATCH /api/nodes/{mac}/position (handler.updateNodePosition -> ForwardNodePosition) and node connect/register (OnNodeConnected forwards the registry position). Commit a17bdd2. Tests: TestHandlerUpdateNodePositionForwardsToEngine (asserts the engine's stored position for a MAC changes after a PATCH with new coords), TestManagerOnNodeConnectedForwardsPositionToEngine (asserts connect seeds the engine position). Existing fleet handler tests still pass; go build/vet/test ./... all green.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Implemented and verified (commit a17bdd2, pushed to origin/main).

The fleet Manager gains a nodePositionSink — the write-side mirror of the read-side SetNodePositionAccessor used by diagnostics/weather.go — wired at startup (main.go) to fusionEngine.SetNodePosition. It fires from both position paths:
- PATCH /api/nodes/{mac}/position: Handler.updateNodePosition calls ForwardNodePosition after the registry accepts the new position.
- Node connect/register: Manager.OnNodeConnected forwards the node's current registry position.

Tests (internal/fleet/handler_test.go):
- TestHandlerUpdateNodePositionForwardsToEngine: asserts the engine's stored position for a MAC changes after a PATCH with new coordinates (seeds 1.5,2.5,3.5 then updates to 7,8,9).
- TestManagerOnNodeConnectedForwardsPositionToEngine: asserts connect pushes the registry position to the engine.

Verification: go build ./... OK, go vet ./... OK, go test ./... all pass (existing fleet handler tests still green).

title: Add GET-based regression test for dashboard static-asset 200 + MIME
description:
Parent: bf-1cgqe. Add a regression test so the 404/text/plain failure (diagnosed in the bf-1cgqe comment) cannot silently recur. Depends on the fallback-fix child.

SCOPE:
- Add a Go test under mothership/cmd/mothership/ (or appropriate _test.go) that exercises the dashboard static handler against the real dashboard/ assets and asserts, via GET:
    GET /css/tokens.css -> 200, Content-Type text/css
    GET /js/app.js      -> 200, Content-Type text/javascript (or application/javascript)
    GET /               -> 200, Content-Type text/html
- The test MUST use GET (httptest.NewRequest with Method GET), NOT HEAD — the route is GET-only and HEAD returns 405, which is exactly what made prior 'curl -sI' checks misleadingly report failure.
- If the handler is hard to isolate, factor a small registerDashboardStatic(r, staticDir) helper that both main.go and the test can call (this is the natural seam).

ACCEPTANCE:
- [ ] New test passes and asserts 200 + correct Content-Type for a .css, a .js, and index.html.
- [ ] Test FAILS if the handler is unregistered or returns text/plain (proves it guards the bug).
- [ ] go test ./... green.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Implemented bf-3uud3 (commit 8fd0786, pushed to origin/main).

Added TestDashboardStaticAssets in mothership/cmd/mothership/dashboard_static_test.go exercising the REAL dashboard/ assets over GET (httptest.NewRequest http.MethodGet — NOT HEAD, since the route is GET-only and HEAD returns 405, the trap that made prior curl -sI checks misleadingly fail):
  GET /css/tokens.css -> 200, Content-Type text/css
  GET /js/app.js      -> 200, Content-Type text/javascript (application/javascript accepted)
  GET /               -> 200, Content-Type text/html

Factored the inline handler out of main() into registerDashboardStatic(r, staticDir) + dashboardStaticHandler(staticDir) so production and the test wire the IDENTICAL handler (drift impossible by construction); main() now calls registerDashboardStatic(r, resolvedDir).

ACCEPTANCE:
[x] New test passes and asserts 200 + correct Content-Type for .css, .js, index.html.
[x] Test FAILS if handler unregistered or returns text/plain — PROVEN by mutation:
    - mutating dashboardStaticHandler to serve text/plain -> main test FAILS on all 3 subtests (Content-Type text/plain; charset=utf-8 rejected)
    - dropping the registration -> main test FAILS with 404 on all 3 subtests
    Both reverted; production code identical to pre-mutation backup.
    Two negative-control tests (GuardUnregistered, GuardRejectsTextPlain) also pin the assertion teeth.
[~] go test ./... : cmd/mothership green + go vet ./cmd/mothership clean. Remaining failures (internal/ble label_test.go build break from an UNTRACKED file, and tests/e2e TestConcurrentNodes/TestDetectionEvents/TestFullE2EIntegration node-rejected/no-blob) reproduce IDENTICALLY on clean HEAD with my changes stashed — pre-existing, unrelated to static serving.

Note: per known br gotcha, closing via br comments add + br close (no --body).

The only go test ./... failure is internal/ble from an UNTRACKED file (mothership/internal/ble/label_test.go, references undefined IdentityMatch.Label) — not part of this change. Closing.

title: Capture baseline and verify presence detection with first node
description:
After bf-2po1 (node connected and streaming CSI):

In passive radar mode (single node, using home AP beacons as TX source):
1. Ensure room is empty, then POST /api/baseline/capture to capture empty-room baseline
   curl -X POST https://spaxel.ardenone.com/api/baseline/capture (auth required)
2. Wait 30s for baseline to stabilize (EMA with tau=30s)
3. Walk through the detection area between the node and the home WiFi AP
4. In the dashboard, observe: deltaRMS should spike from ~0.02 to ~0.10+
5. A blob should appear in the 3D visualization at the detected position

With a single node, only presence detection is reliable (not precise localization).
For localization, a second node on the opposite side of the space is needed.

Success: deltaRMS > 0.05 when person is present, blob visible in dashboard.

Troubleshooting: if no signal change, check passive_bss NVS key has the AP BSSID,
or switch node to TX_RX mode and use node-to-node CSI instead of passive radar.

title: Verify dashboard JS/TS identity-field surface
description:
## Objective
Verify the dashboard JS/TS blob surface declares and correctly surfaces the three canonical identity fields (personName, assignedColor, identityResolved), aligning field names with the Go JSON tags emitted by the backend projections. This closes the Tier-4 dashboard track of notes/bf-1q3m-consolidated.md §6 (#7/#8/#9).

## Scope (grounded in notes/bf-1q3m-consolidated.md §3.4 + §6 Tier-4)
- Confirm `dashboard/types/spaxel.d.ts` Blob interface (lines 10-91) declares `personName`, `assignedColor`, `identityResolved` (preliminary grep shows all three present at lines 53/74/90 — verify exact, including the deprecated personLabel/personColor aliases).
- Cross-check the TS field names against the Go JSON tags emitted by the projections that feed the dashboard: `dashboard.blobJSON` (internal/dashboard/hub.go), `api.Track` (internal/api/tracks.go), and — once children 1 & 2 land — `explainability.BlobSnapshot` and `volume.BlobPos`. Document the mapping; adjust TS or Go tags only if a real mismatch is found.
- Confirm `dashboard/js/state.js:290` creation literal (`appState.blobs[id] = { id: id }`) correctly surfaces server-supplied identity via the `Object.assign(...)` at state.js:292 — no pre-init needed if the server sends the fields (it does, post-Tier-1).
- `dashboard/js/websocket.js:167` (J2 dead-reckoning cache) is intentionally identity-free — confirm and leave alone.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] spaxel.d.ts Blob interface confirmed to declare personName, assignedColor, identityResolved (+ deprecated aliases)
- [ ] TS field names ↔ Go JSON-tag mapping documented; any mismatch fixed
- [ ] state.js:290 creation path confirmed to surface server identity via Object.assign; no change needed unless broken

Cross-checked TS vs Go JSON tags across all 8 blob-shaped types: 7 already camelCase; FIXED volume.BlobPos — the sole outlier emitting snake_case (person_name/assigned_color/identity_resolved -> camelCase) to match the canonical convention (bf-5151) and the dashboard. Tags were inert (volume.BlobPos is consumed in Go by Store.Evaluate, which returns fired-trigger IDs; never marshalled to the dashboard) but would have leaked snake_case if ever surfaced. state.js:290 surfaces server identity via Object.assign (no change needed); websocket.js:167 dead-reckoning cache intentionally identity-free (left alone). go vet ./... and go test ./... pass. description:

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Closed by verification. Position propagation through the full chain (simulator VirtualNodeStore -> registry_bridge -> fleet.Registry SQLite -> fusion.Engine.nodePos mirror -> accumulation grid) was already wired by prior children (bf-5lii -> bf-69ym -> bf-u7ds -> bf-95tx).

This bead's acceptance criterion -- 'positions reach the fusion engine accumulation grid and actually drive localization' -- is locked in by TestPositionPropagation_ReachesAccumulationGrid in mothership/internal/api/simulator_test.go. It posts two explicitly-positioned nodes via the simulator REST API, runs one Fuse step with a link between the two MACs the engine now holds, and asserts a blob localizes near the link midpoint (confidence 1.0, 1 active link). That peak is only reachable if BOTH endpoints' positions reached the grid lookup (AddLinkInfluence reads nodePos[mac] for both endpoints; a missing/co-located position yields no peak).

Verified: go test ./... PASS (internal/api incl.), go vet ./... clean. Grid trace under -v:
  [grid] position propagation  midpoint=(3.00,1.00)  peak=(2.90,0.90,0.90) conf=1.000 activeLinks=1

No production wiring change needed -- prior children completed it. Committed and pushed to origin/main.

comments.author: cli

The wiring from prior children is complete and correct: a posted node position flows VirtualNodeStore → fleetRegistryBridge.SyncToRegistry (SQLite write) → fleetRegistryAdapter forwardPos hook → fleetMgr.ForwardNodePosition → nodePositionSink → fusion.Engine.SetNodePosition, and Fuse reads nodePos[mac] for both endpoints into AddLinkInfluence on the accumulation grid. No production change was needed. Lock-in: TestPositionPropagation_ReachesAccumulationGrid posts two explicitly-positioned nodes through the simulator REST API and asserts one Fuse step localizes a blob near the link midpoint (peak=(2.90,0.90,0.90), conf=1.000, activeLinks=1) — only reachable if BOTH endpoints' positions reached the grid lookup (a missing/co-located position yields no peak, cf. TestEngine_CoLocatedOriginYieldsNoPeaks). go vet ./... clean; go test ./... fully green.

title: Build and boot the mothership hardware-free to /healthz
description:
Decomposed from bf-40hc (hardware-free runtime blob path). First link in the chain: prove the mothership can be built and started WITHOUT any ESP32 hardware and reach a healthy state.

The exact path is already exercised in mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go (TestHarness.Start) — use it as the source of truth.

## Scope
- From the mothership module root (mothership/, module github.com/spaxel/mothership; go.work declares it as the root), build:
  go build -o /tmp/spaxel-mothership ./cmd/mothership
- Run with env: SPAXEL_BIND_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8080, SPAXEL_DATA_DIR=<tmp dir>, SPAXEL_LOG_LEVEL=info, TZ=UTC
- Confirm GET http://localhost:8080/healthz returns {"status":"ok",...} within ~15s

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Build command documented and succeeds from a clean checkout.
- [ ] Mothership starts with the documented env and reaches /healthz status=ok with no runtime panic/crash in stderr.
- [ ] Exact commands written into the bead body for the next child to reuse.
close_reason: Completed — all 3 acceptance criteria verified (build exits 0; /healthz status=ok in ~1s; no panic/crash in stderr). failure-count:3 were 10-min wall-clock timeouts on already-succeeded work, not work failures. Recipe in comment [106] + PROGRESS.md.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Hardware-free mothership build + boot to /healthz — DONE (verified 2026-07-07).

EXACT COMMANDS for the next child in the bf-40hc chain (reusable as-is):

# 1. Build (from clean checkout; no -tags=embed needed locally):
cd mothership                          # module root: github.com/spaxel/mothership
go build -o /tmp/spaxel-mothership ./cmd/mothership

# 2. Boot to /healthz (no ESP32, no Docker):
DATADIR=$(mktemp -d /tmp/spaxel-data-XXXX)
SPAXEL_BIND_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8080 SPAXEL_DATA_DIR=$DATADIR SPAXEL_LOG_LEVEL=info TZ=UTC /tmp/spaxel-mothership &

# 3. Verify (reaches ok in <1s; ~15s budget):

title: Establish a hardware-free runtime path that creates a blob
description:
## Objective
Decomposed from parent bf-f841 (Test blob identity fields at runtime). This first step pins down a reliable, hardware-free way to RUN the mothership and produce a tracked blob, which the original bead never specified — that ambiguity caused the repeated failures.

## Scope
- Use cmd/sim (spaxel-sim) or a documented local mothership launch to run WITHOUT ESP32 hardware.
- Drive enough simulated CSI to make the tracker emit at least one tracked blob at runtime.
- Capture the exact reproduce command(s) so later steps (and reruns) are deterministic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Documented command starts the mothership without a runtime panic/crash.
- [ ] At least one blob object is observable at runtime (via API, WebSocket, or tracker log).
- [ ] Reproduce command written into the bead body or a referenced note.

title: Document, commit, and close the verified geometric fix for bf-39mli
description:
## Objective
FOURTH (final) child of the bf-39mli split. Record the verified root cause + fix, commit ONLY the files that belong to this bead, and close bf-39mli. This child is deliberately isolated because the final commit/close step is the one most prone to getting stuck on br close failures and re-triggering auto-split.

## Depends on
bf-5mlqh (verified PASS + no regression + reverted unverified changes).

## Reuse (do NOT re-derive)
- Close syntax: br close <id> does NOT accept --body (auto-dispatch templates that say br close --body are WRONG for this br build). Attach the summary as a comment first, then close with no body flag:
  1. br comments add bf-39mli "<summary>"
  2. br close bf-39mli
  3. br sync --flush-only
- If br close errors premature end of input, the cause is naive worker_sessions.claimed_at timestamps from timeout dispatches; repair with system sqlite3 (nix) NOT br doctor:
  UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=REPLACE(claimed_at,' ','T')||'Z' WHERE bead_id='bf-39mli' AND length(claimed_at)=19;  then retry br close.
- Commit ONLY the bead-scoped files (mothership/cmd/sim/main.go geometry, mothership/internal/ble/identity.go [BLEDBG] instrumentation + any verified geometry). Use explicit git add paths. EXCLUDE .beads/issues.jsonl, .beads/traces/*, cmd/sim/sim binary, notes/hardware-free-runtime.md (belongs to bf-40hc).
- Never force-push. git push to origin is pre-approved if reversible.

## Scope
- Post a summary comment on bf-39mli stating: the failing gate (f_distance), the verified geometric root cause, the one minimal fix applied, and the PASS evidence (blob_id + confidence).
- Commit with a message like: fix(bf-39mli): co-locate Alice CSI blob with triangulated point so matcher clears f_distance
- br comments add + br close bf-39mli (no --body) + br sync --flush-only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Summary comment posted on bf-39mli with root cause + fix + PASS evidence.
- [ ] Focused commit landed (bead-scoped files only; unrelated diffs left out).
- [ ] bf-39mli closed; br show bf-39mli shows status closed.
- [ ] br sync --flush-only run (checkpoint the close).

title: Document the window-independent auth policy in PROGRESS.md, commit, and close bf-4mle6
description:

title: Dockerfile: bust Kaniko cache for firmware stage to force 4MB rebuild
description:
The 0.1.352 Docker image contains firmware compiled with CONFIG_ESPTOOLPY_FLASHSIZE_16MB (16MB header). The fix was already committed in d837598 (sdkconfig.defaults -> 4MB, partitions.csv redesigned for 4MB). But Kaniko served a cached firmware layer for the 0.1.352 build, ignoring the sdkconfig.defaults change.

Symptom: ESP32-S3 (4MB flash) flashed via Web Serial crashes on every boot with:
  spi_flash: Detected size(4096k) smaller than binary image header(16384k). Probe failed.
  assert failed: do_core_init startup.c:326

Fix:
1. In firmware stage of Dockerfile, add a cache-busting ARG before the COPY:
   ARG FIRMWARE_CACHE_BUST=2026-06-03
   This guarantees Kaniko treats the layer as new on the next build.

2. Also add RUN rm -f sdkconfig sdkconfig.old after COPY and before idf.py set-target
   so any stale tracked sdkconfig cannot survive into the build.

After this change, idf.py set-target will regenerate sdkconfig from sdkconfig.defaults
which has CONFIG_ESPTOOLPY_FLASHSIZE_4MB=y, producing firmware with a 4MB header.

Reference: firmware/sdkconfig.defaults (CONFIG_ESPTOOLPY_FLASHSIZE_4MB=y)
           firmware/partitions.csv (4MB layout: factory+ota_0 fit in 4MB)
close_reason: Fix was already committed in d837598/301a588 (Kaniko cache-bust ARG + sdkconfig cleanup in the firmware Dockerfile stage) weeks ago. Bead was stuck retrying due to a dead ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL in the claude-code-glm-5 adapter (fixed 2026-07-02); the agent correctly verified the fix was in place and closed this itself, but a missing verify-changes.sh hook on lab (also fixed 2026-07-02) reopened it every time. No further action needed.

Verified: firmware/sdkconfig.defaults has CONFIG_ESPTOOLPY_FLASHSIZE_4MB=y, firmware/partitions.csv is a 4MB layout (factory+ota_0 fit), VERSION already at 0.1.357 (past the 0.1.354 bump that accompanied this fix), so a fresh CI build producing the 4MB-header firmware has already run. Resolves the ESP32-S3 boot crash (spi_flash detected 4096k < binary header 16384k).

title: Run spaxel-sim and prove frames/s > 0
description:
Third step of the chain (depends on the no-REJECT child). With the binary built and tokenless nodes accepted, run spaxel-sim against the healthy mothership (bf-3zll) and prove it streams synthetic CSI.

## Scope
- Start the hardware-free mothership (per bf-3zll) and wait for /healthz = ok.
- Run the documented command:
  /tmp/spaxel-sim --mothership ws://localhost:8080/ws/node --nodes 4 --walkers 1 --rate 20 --duration 30 --ble --seed 42
- Confirm all 4 nodes connect and the sim streams frames.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 4 nodes connect (sim logs report 4 connected nodes, no REJECT).
- [ ] Sim end-of-run stats report frames/s > 0.
- [ ] Exact run command recorded in the bead body.

title: Add test coverage for geometry placement and fusion peaks
description:
Child of bf-18yn (split). Depends on bf-3y9r.

Write tests that verify the geometry placement produces meaningful fusion results.

Scope:
- Add a sim or host test that creates virtual nodes with default placement
- Verify the fusion accumulation grid produces non-zero peaks (not all zeros)
- Ensure test would fail with old co-located placement but passes with new geometry
- Run existing simulator tests to ensure no regressions

Acceptance criteria:
- New test passes with realistic geometry
- Test verifies fusion accumulation grid has non-zero peaks
- Existing simulator tests still pass
- go build ./... passes

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Implemented (commit c4d42e8, pushed to main). Added TestEngine_GeometryPlacementDrivesFusionPeaks to mothership/internal/fusion/fusion_test.go — a differential lock-in that holds node placement as the SOLE variable (engine grid, all-pairs motion link set, and room identical across both legs) and proves the non-zero-peak condition flips with geometry: default (spread) placement accumulates a non-zero fusion grid (gridMax>0; blobs>=1 for count>=4 where crossing links form a true maximum), while the old co-located (0,0,1) collapse makes every link degenerate (<0.1m), so AddLinkInfluence early-returns and the grid stays at zero (gridMax==0, 0 blobs). The trace shows the flip: spread gridMax=1.0 blobs=2 vs colocated gridMax=0.0 blobs=0. Gates: go test ./... , go vet ./... , and go build ./... (mothership + cmd/sim + test/acceptance) all pass.

- go vet ./... clean across all 3 modules
- go test ./... — all packages pass (mothership incl. internal/fusion + internal/simulator, test/acceptance ok)
- New test passes with realistic geometry: spread placement accumulates a non-zero grid (gridMax=1.0) and count>=4 crossing links extract >=1 blob (nodes=4 -> blobs=2, conf=0.768)
- Differential lock-in holds placement as the SOLE variable (identical engine grid, all-pairs motion link set, room): spread gridMax=1.0 blobs=2 vs old co-located (0,0,1) collapse gridMax=0.0 blobs=0 — the non-zero-peak condition flips with geometry, proving the test is genuinely regression-sensitive to the bf-4q5w collapse.

title: Verify live dashboard console is clean with identity-less blobs
description:
## Objective
Final end-to-end acceptance for parent bf-2gmx: serve the real dashboard against blob data that LACKS identity fields and confirm the browser console is clean — i.e. true backward compatibility is observed at runtime, not just in unit/jsdom tests.

## Context
Depends on the runtime test child (split-child) which proved the renderer is safe headlessly. This child confirms the same in the live app. Recent identity-blob runtime work (bf-15oi "zero errors", bf-gdfwx capstone PASS on /api/blobs) is the identity-RESOLVED path; this child is the complementary NOT-resolved path.

## Scope
- Run the local sim WITHOUT --ble (per run-sim-local / hardware-free-runtime docs) so /api/blobs serves blobs with NO identity fields
- Serve the dashboard and load it (browser or headless e.g. the project's verify/run approach)
- Capture the console; confirm zero errors mentioning personName/assignedColor/identityResolved or undefined access
- Confirm blobs still render visibly (fallback color) AND that identity-resolved blobs (if mixed in) render with identity

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dashboard loads against identity-less /api/blobs data with NO console errors
- [ ] Captured console log saved (docs note / trace) showing no identity-related undefined errors
- [ ] Identity-less blobs render with a fallback color; identity-resolved blobs render with personName/assignedColor
- [ ] Backward compatibility confirmed end-to-end — record PASS summary as a comment on bf-2gmx

title: Update ardenone-cluster deployment to spaxel:0.1.354 after CI

title: Re-flash ESP32 via Web Serial after 4MB firmware is deployed
description:
Prerequisites: bf-4eal (deployment to 0.1.354) is complete.

Steps:
1. Open Chrome or Edge (Web Serial requires Chromium-based browser)
2. Navigate to spaxel.ardenone.com (auth required — use existing credentials)
3. Go to the provision / onboard page
4. Connect the ESP32-S3 dev board via USB-C
5. Click Flash/Provision — esptool.js will download firmware from mothership
   and write it to the device
6. Monitor the serial output: should boot, NOT crash with flash size mismatch
7. Expected: captive portal or WiFi connection attempt (node is unprovisioned)

If the device still shows the 16MB crash, check /api/firmware/manifest returns
a firmware binary whose image header byte[3] bits 4-7 = 0x4 (4MB), not 0x6 (16MB).

This is the validation step that confirms the 4MB fix works end-to-end.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: SOFTWARE PRE-CHECK VERIFIED (agent, no hardware): The deployed firmware binary is confirmed 4MB, not 16MB. Pulled ronaldraygun/spaxel:0.1.358 (matches running instance version per /healthz) and extracted /firmware/spaxel-firmware.bin (1.62MB merged binary). APP image header @0x10000 byte[3]=0x2F -> high nibble 0x2 = 4MB (esptool encoding). Bootloader header @0x0 byte[3]=0x2F = 4MB. An old buggy 16MB build would be 0x4F (nibble 0x4). Fix source confirmed in HEAD: commit d837598 (sdkconfig 16MB->4MB, partitions.csv 4MB redesign, onboard.js flashSize 'detect'->'4MB'); Dockerfile builds with idf.py 4MB config + esptool merge_bin --flash_size 4MB. HEADS-UP on this bead's stated nibbles: it says 'bits4-7=0x4(4MB),0x6(16MB)' but that does NOT match esptool's standard mapping (0x2=4MB,0x4=16MB,0x6=64MB). Don't check for literal 0x4 - the deployed 4MB binary is 0x2. REMAINING (needs human + ESP32-S3 + Chrome/Edge): the actual Web Serial flash + serial boot observation (steps 1-7). Agent cannot perform physical USB flashing, so bead NOT closed.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
RE-VERIFIED (independent, this dispatch 2026-07-02) — leaving OPEN.

(1) DEPLOYED FIRMWARE BYTE-VERIFIED 4MB (independent of prior comment #19). Pulled ronaldraygun/spaxel:0.1.358 (exact version running per /healthz: status=ok, uptime~686819s/~7.95d, db=ok, shedding_level=0). Extracted /firmware/spaxel-firmware.bin (1,696,480 B / ~1.62MB, built 2026-06-27). ESP image headers:
  - Bootloader @0x0:     E9 04 02 2F -> magic 0xE9 OK; byte[3]=0x2F -> flash-size nibble(bits4-7)=0x2 = 4MB
  - App       @0x10000:  E9 05 02 2F -> byte[3]=0x2F -> 4MB
  A buggy 16MB build would be 0x4F (nibble 0x4). Deployed binary is genuinely 4MB. CAVEAT (same as #19): the bead's stated nibbles '0x4=4MB, 0x6=16MB' do NOT match esptool's standard map (0x2=4MB, 0x4=16MB, 0x6=64MB); under the real map the deployed 4MB binary is nibble 0x2.

(2) SOURCE-OF-TRUTH corroborates: firmware/sdkconfig CONFIG_ESPTOOLPY_FLASHSIZE_4MB=y (16MB not set); partitions.csv = 4MB layout (factory 0x1F0000 + ota_0 0x1F0000 + otadata 0x3F0000); fix commit 301a588 in HEAD; deployed 0.1.358 newer than fix version 0.1.354.

(3) PHYSICAL STEP STILL UNREACHABLE (re-confirmed, permanent): no /dev/ttyUSB* or /dev/ttyACM*; no serial/ESP device in lsusb; esptool not installed; no Chromium; and Web Serial is inherently a human-granted interactive browser permission over a physically-USB-connected device. No agent environment can do steps 1-7.

DECISION: OPEN. All agent-verifiable criteria (the 4MB-header precondition) are satisfied and byte-confirmed, but the bead's actual purpose -- end-to-end validation by physically flashing an ESP32-S3 and observing boot -- has NOT been done by any agent or human. bf-4gbf gates the physical sub-chain (next: bf-2po1 provision the node, also hardware-only); closing on a software-only check would falsely signal 'device flashed & ready' and unblock hardware-dependent beads for a device that does not exist here. Needs a human + USB-C + ESP32-S3 + Chrome/Edge. No code changes; fix already deployed in 0.1.358.

comments.text: IMPORTANT for whoever does the physical flash: the task's stated header test is INVERTED vs the real esptool spec. The task says 'byte[3] bits 4-7 = 0x4 (4MB), not 0x6 (16MB)'. Correct esptool encoding: 4MB -> image-header byte[3] high-nibble 0x2 (full byte 0x20); 16MB -> high-nibble 0x4 (full byte 0x40). So a correctly-built 4MB image is EXPECTED to read 0x2 in that nibble, not 0x4. Verify with: esptool.py --port /dev/ttyUSB0 image_info spaxel-*.bin and check 'Flash size: 4MB', or hexdump byte[3] of the .bin and confirm high nibble is 0x2. If the device still shows a flash-size mismatch crash after flashing, that is the real thing to investigate.

title: Resolve the REJECT/token path so tokenless sim nodes are accepted
description:
Second step of the chain (depends on bf-3ud7e build). With the binary built, ensure the hardware-free mothership (bf-3zll) does NOT reject tokenless spaxel-sim nodes.

Source of truth: mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go ~lines 506-525. If a token validator is configured (SetTokenValidator) AND the migration window is closed (SetMigrationDeadline zero or past), every tokenless sim node is rejected via sendReject with reason 'invalid_token' and disconnected — this is the 'REJECT'/401/403 failure mode.

## Scope
- Determine whether the local/test mothership wires a token validator and what the migration deadline is.
- If a validator is active for the sim run, disable it (unset validator), open the migration window, or have the sim supply valid tokens — whichever is correct for the hardware-free build.
- Verify by running the sim (1-2 nodes) and grepping mothership stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root cause of any REJECT identified (validator configured? migration window closed?).
- [ ] Mothership configured so tokenless sim nodes connect without rejection.
- [ ] When the sim connects, mothership stderr shows zero occurrences of 'rejected' / 'REJECT' / 'invalid_token' / HTTP 401 / 403.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
[bf-34lwt root-cause] TOKENLESS-SIM REJECT PATH — definitive finding (citations are full repo-relative paths; the task body's cmd/* maps to mothership/cmd/*).

TL;DR: current 'no reject' is a 24h MIGRATION-WINDOW MASK, not a fix. The validator is wired unconditionally, but the sim presents its token only as an HTTP header the mothership never reads, so hello.Token is always empty and tokenOK is always false. The only thing keeping sim nodes connected is the default 24h window; the moment it closes, every sim node is sendReject-ed (invalid_token) and disconnected.

1) Validator wired UNCONDITIONALLY: mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go:4494 ingestSrv.SetTokenValidator(provSrv.ValidateToken) — no config/build gate. Only the *deadline* is gated (main.go:4495 if cfg.MigrationWindowHours > 0). So s.tokenValidator is always non-nil. => 'validator configured?' is always YES.

2) Validator reads hello.Token only: mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:513 tokenOK := hello.Token != "" && validator(hello.MAC, hello.Token). validator = ValidateToken(mac,token) (SetTokenValidator at server.go:321-327). hello.Token is JSON 'token,omitempty' (message.go:22).

3) Sim sends token ONLY as X-Spaxel-Token HTTP header, NOT in hello body: mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:633 + mothership/cmd/sim/scenario.go:318 headers.Set("X-Spaxel-Token", token). Hello body map (main.go:652-665 / scenario.go:327-340) has type/mac/firmware_version/capabilities/chip/flash_mb/uptime_ms/wifi_rssi/ip/pos_* — NO 'token' key => hello.Token deserializes to "".

4) Mothership NEVER reads that header => token-supply path is DEAD. grep 'X-Spaxel-Token' mothership/ returns ONLY sim files (cmd/sim/*); zero hits in cmd/mothership or internal. The WS upgrade handler reads nothing off the request: server.go:469 s.upgrader.Upgrade(w, r, nil) inside HandleNodeWS (server.go:455) — no r.Header.Get. (All other Header.Get/Set under internal/ are outbound webhook/notify or tests.) Consequence: hello.Token == "" for every sim node => tokenOK always false, regardless of the provisioned token. The parent's 'have the sim supply valid tokens' option is currently NON-FUNCTIONAL.

5) Only the migration window accepts sim nodes: server.go:515 if !deadline.IsZero() && time.Now().Before(deadline) => nc.Unpaired = true. MigrationWindowHours DEFAULTS to 24 (config.go:139). Fresh boot sets deadline=now+24h (main.go:4496) => window open => sim nodes accepted as Unpaired, no reject. Matches PROGRESS.md bf-3hji.

6) REJECT fires ONLY when window closed. Two cases: (a) SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0 => main.go:4495 false => SetMigrationDeadline never called => deadline IsZero() => server.go:515 !IsZero() false => reject branch; (b) uptime > 24h => Now().Before(deadline) false => reject branch. Either way hello.Token=="" => server.go:521 'missing token' => server.go:525 sendReject(conn,"invalid_token") => server.go:526 conn.Close() (sendReject at server.go:841-846).

EXPLICIT STATEMENTS (acceptance criteria):
- Current 'no reject' is a 24h-window MASK, not a real fix: it vanishes under SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0 or after 24h uptime.
- The token-supply path is currently DEAD: header ignored, hello.Token empty, tokenOK always false.

Full write-up + fix options (A keep-mask / B make-token-real via header-read-in-HandleNodeWS-or-body-token / C gate-the-validator): docs/notes/token-reject-root-cause.md (committed with bf-34lwt). The remaining bf-4iewr children should pick exactly one of A/B/C; the two invariants for the final state: 'no reject' must not depend on the 24h mask, and the chosen token contract must be honored end-to-end by both sim and mothership.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE (bf-34lwt, first link of this split) — confirmed against current source. Full write-up: docs/notes/token-reject-root-cause.md (commit 5c6a692).

All module paths are under mothership/; the task body dropped that prefix.

1. VALIDATOR WIRED UNCONDITIONALLY: mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go:4494 ingestSrv.SetTokenValidator(provSrv.ValidateToken) — no config/build gate, so 'validator configured?' is always YES. Only the deadline is gated (main.go:4495 if cfg.MigrationWindowHours > 0).

2. VALIDATOR READS hello.Token ONLY: mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:513 tokenOK := hello.Token != "" && validator(hello.MAC, hello.Token). ValidateToken (mothership/internal/provisioning/server.go:135) does an HMAC constant-time compare on the token STRING — it never touches any HTTP header. hello.Token maps to JSON field token,omitempty (mothership/internal/ingestion/message.go:22).

3. SIM SENDS TOKEN ONLY AS X-Spaxel-Token HEADER, never in the hello body. Both sim copies: mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:633 and mothership/cmd/sim/scenario.go:318 set headers.Set("X-Spaxel-Token", token) on the WS dial; standalone module cmd/sim/main.go:313 does the same. None of the hello bodies include a 'token' key (mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:652-665; cmd/sim/main.go:348-359; scenario.go:331-341), so hello.Token deserializes to "" from either binary.

4. MOTHERSHIP NEVER READS THE HEADER -> TOKEN-SUPPLY PATH IS DEAD. grep 'X-Spaxel-Token' over mothership/ returns only sim files; the WS upgrade handler upgrades UNCONDITIONALLY at server.go:469 s.upgrader.Upgrade(w, r, nil) with no r.Header.Get(...) and no HTTP-401 path (HandleNodeWS, server.go:455). So hello.Token=="" -> server.go:513 short-circuits to tokenOK=false regardless of the provisioned token. The 'have the sim supply valid tokens' option in THIS bead is therefore currently NON-FUNCTIONAL — the token goes to a header nobody reads.

5. THE ONLY THING ACCEPTING SIM NODES IS THE MIGRATION WINDOW. server.go:514-519: if !tokenOK, accept as Unpaired only when !deadline.IsZero() && Now().Before(deadline). MigrationWindowHours DEFAULTS TO 24 (mothership/internal/config/config.go:139), so a fresh boot sets deadline=now+24h (main.go:4496) -> every tokenless sim node accepted as Unpaired, no reject (matches PROGRESS.md bf-3hji). Corroborated by the e2e harness itself (mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go:86-94), which pins SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=24 and documents exactly this.

6. REJECT FIRES ONLY WHEN THE WINDOW IS CLOSED (server.go:519-528): (a) SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0 -> SetMigrationDeadline never called (main.go:4495 guard) -> deadline.IsZero() true -> reject; (b) uptime > 24h -> Now().Before(deadline) false -> reject. In both, hello.Token=="" logs 'Node <MAC> rejected: missing token' (server.go:520-521), sendReject(conn,"invalid_token") (server.go:525, def 841-846), conn.Close() (526).

BONUS: there is NO HTTP-401 path at all — the upgrade always succeeds (101). So AS-7 quality gate #7 ('node without token must get HTTP 401 and be rejected') is currently UNSATISFIABLE under default config; it's part of the same dead-path/accident.

SUMMARY (for this bead's acceptance criteria):
- 'No reject' is a 24h-window MASK, not a real fix — it vanishes under SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0 or after 24h uptime.
- The token-supply path is currently DEAD: header ignored (server.go:469), hello.Token empty (sim hello bodies), so tokenOK is always false (server.go:513).
- Three independent fixes for the sibling children: (A) keep the mask but make it deliberate for sim/test; (B) make the token path real (read X-Spaxel-Token in HandleNodeWS, or have the sim put token in the hello body); (C) gate the validator off for the hardware-free build. See the doc for the trade-offs.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
## Root cause: tokenless-sim REJECT in the WS hello path (bf-34lwt synthesis)

Synthesis of the three bf-34lwt children (bf-5ig3e, bf-29wyl, bf-2hdbg). All six facts re-confirmed against current source (repo-relative paths):

**1. Validator is wired UNCONDITIONALLY** — `mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go:4494`
`ingestSrv.SetTokenValidator(provSrv.ValidateToken)` runs at every boot with NO env/build guard, so "is a validator configured?" is always YES. (The migration deadline at lines 4495-4498 is wired separately, only when `MigrationWindowHours > 0`.)

**2. Validator reads `hello.Token` ONLY** — `mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:513`
`tokenOK := hello.Token != "" && validator(hello.MAC, hello.Token)`. Accept/reject hinges entirely on the JSON body's `token` field; no HTTP header participates in the check.

**3. Sim sends its token ONLY as the `X-Spaxel-Token` header** — `cmd/sim/main.go:313`
`reqHeader.Set("X-Spaxel-Token", token)`. The hello map (`cmd/sim/main.go:348-359`) carries type/mac/firmware_version/capabilities/chip/flash_mb/uptime_ms/pos_{x,y,z} and NO `token` field. `cmd/sim/scenario.go` sets no header or body token either.

**4. Mothership NEVER reads that header** — `grep -rn 'X-Spaxel-Token' mothership/cmd/mothership/ mothership/internal/` returns zero hits; every `Header.Get` under `mothership/internal/` is an outbound notification client (webhook/ntfy/pushover/mqtt), none on the WS node path. Consequence: for sim nodes `hello.Token` is always `""` -> `tokenOK` is always false.

**5. The migration window — not tokens — is the only thing accepting sim nodes** — `mothership/internal/config/config.go:139` (`cfg.MigrationWindowHours = 24`, the default), applied at `mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go:4495-4498` to set a deadline 24h out. The acceptance branch is `mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:515`: `if !deadline.IsZero() && time.Now().Before(deadline)` -> node admitted and flagged `Unpaired` (matches PROGRESS.md bf-3hji).

**6. REJECT fires ONLY when the window is closed** — `mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:519-527`, the else branch: logs "missing token"/"invalid token" -> `sendReject(conn, "invalid_token")` -> `conn.Close()`. Two triggers: (a) `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0` -> the `main.go:4495` gate is skipped -> deadline stays zero-value -> `deadline.IsZero()` true -> reject; (b) uptime > 24h -> `time.Now().After(deadline)` -> reject.

## Two required takeaways

- **"No reject" is a 24h-window MASK, not a real fix.** A default-boot mothership rejects nothing only because the 24h migration window is open. The instant it closes (`SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0`, or any process alive >24h), every tokenless sim node hits the `invalid_token` reject branch. The hardware-free build is green by accident of uptime, not by design.

- **The token-supply path is currently DEAD.** The parent bead's "supply valid tokens" option is non-functional today: the sim's token lives only in the unread `X-Spaxel-Token` header, while the validator checks only the always-empty `hello.Token` body field — so even a correctly-provisioned token cannot make `tokenOK` true.

## Precise target for the remaining bf-4iewr children

Repair the dead token-supply path (point 4): either (a) populate `hello.Token` in the sim's hello body, or (b) have the mothership read `X-Spaxel-Token` on the WS upgrade. Opening/lengthening the migration window (points 5-6) only hides the bug and must not be treated as the fix.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
[bf-34lwt — first link] REJECT/token ROOT CAUSE in the WS hello path (definitive, re-verified at HEAD 402bb7b on 2026-07-07).

PURPOSE: give the remaining bf-4iewr children a precise target. The bead is currently PASSING-BY-ACCIDENT (no reject on a default boot). This comment explains exactly WHY there is no reject today, and exactly WHEN it breaks. All six points below confirmed against current source with file:line (paths are repo-relative; the bead framed them module-relative under mothership/).

1. The token validator is wired UNCONDITIONALLY — there is no config/build gate, so "validator configured?" is always YES.
   - mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go:4494  `ingestSrv.SetTokenValidator(provSrv.ValidateToken)`
   - provSrv is always constructed just above at main.go:4492; SetTokenValidator is called with no guard. At runtime server.go stores it in s.tokenValidator, so the `if validator != nil` check at server.go:512 ALWAYS enters. "Validator configured?" = always YES.

2. The validator reads hello.Token ONLY (the message-BODY field), not the HTTP header.
   - mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:513  `tokenOK := hello.Token != "" && validator(hello.MAC, hello.Token)`
   - hello.Token maps the JSON field `token,omitempty` — mothership/internal/ingestion/message.go:22 `Token string json:"token,omitempty"`. Nothing in this path consults request headers.

3. The sim sends its provisioned token ONLY as the X-Spaxel-Token HTTP header and does NOT put a token in the hello JSON body.
   - mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:633  `headers.Set("X-Spaxel-Token", token)` (main connect path; dial at :637 passes `headers`)
   - mothership/cmd/sim/scenario.go:318  `headers.Set("X-Spaxel-Token", token)` (reconnect path; dial at :320)
   - (alias: the standalone top-level module cmd/sim/main.go:313 sets the same header)
   - The hello BODY the sim marshals (mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:652-665, and scenario.go:331+) carries type/mac/firmware_version/capabilities/chip/flash_mb/uptime_ms/wifi_rssi/ip/pos_x/pos_y/pos_z — and NO `token` key. So on the wire, hello.Token == "" for every sim node.

4. The mothership NEVER reads that header on the WS path. Two facts:
   - `X-Spaxel-Token` has ZERO matches anywhere in mothership/cmd/mothership/ or mothership/internal/ (production or test) — repo-wide the only hits are the sim setting it and test-file comments.
   - `Header.Get` does appear elsewhere in mothership/internal/, but EXCLUSIVELY in *_test.go files and outbound notification/webhook/MQTT clients (reading their own Content-Type / X-Spaxel-Event / notification headers). In the WS handler itself, mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go's only Header reference is `w.Header().Set(...)` at server.go:463 (writing a 503 response), never reading a node request header. So there is NO WS-path read of the token header.
   CONSEQUENCE: hello.Token is always "" for sim nodes → tokenOK at server.go:513 is always false. This is the dead token-supply path: the sim has a token, attaches it as a header, and that header is silently dropped — it never reaches the only place tokens are checked. Therefore the parent bead's "have the sim supply valid tokens" option is currently NON-FUNCTIONAL until either (a) the sim writes its token into the hello body field, or (b) the mothership starts reading the X-Spaxel-Token header on the WS upgrade.

5. The ONLY thing admitting sim nodes today is the migration window.
   - mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:515  `if !deadline.IsZero() && time.Now().Before(deadline)` — this is the acceptance branch; a tokenless node is admitted as Unpaired whenever line 515 holds, regardless of tokenOK.
   - MigrationWindowHours DEFAULTS TO 24 — mothership/internal/config/config.go:139  `cfg.MigrationWindowHours = 24` (default before env override; field comment "default 24, 0 = disabled"). On boot the deadline is computed and installed at mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go:4495-4497 (`if cfg.MigrationWindowHours > 0 { deadline := now + window; ingestSrv.SetMigrationDeadline(deadline) }`). Fresh boot + default 24 → deadline = now+24h → line 515 true for 24h → sim nodes accepted as Unpaired, no reject. This is exactly the bf-3hji "no reject" result.

6. REJECT therefore fires ONLY when the window is closed. The reject branch is the `else` of line 515 — server.go:519-526: it logs "missing token" (hello.Token == "") and calls sendReject(conn, "invalid_token") at server.go:525, then closes the connection (sendReject writes `{"type":"reject","reason":"invalid_token"}`, server.go:841-845). Line 515 is false in exactly two cases:
   - SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0 → main.go:4495 guard is false → SetMigrationDeadline never called → migrationDeadline stays at its zero value → deadline.IsZero() is true → line 515 false → reject branch (strict enforcement from startup); OR
   - mothership uptime > 24h → time.Now().Before(deadline) is false → line 515 false → reject branch.
   In BOTH cases every tokenless sim node hits server.go:525 and is disconnected.

EXPLICIT CONCLUSIONS (the two required statements):

(A) Current "no reject" is a 24h-window MASK, not a real fix. The green state is entirely a property of the default 24h migration window a fresh boot opens (config.go:139 → main.go:4495-4497 → server.go:515). The acceptance decision at server.go:515 is gated purely on the deadline, NOT on token validity — tokenOK is false for every sim node and is simply ignored while the window is open. REJECT returns the instant the window closes: set SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0, or let the mothership run past 24h of uptime, and every tokenless sim node hits sendReject(conn,"invalid_token") at server.go:525 and is disconnected.

(B) The token-supply path is currently DEAD. The sim's token is sent only as the X-Spaxel-Token header (sim main.go:633 / scenario.go:318), which the mothership never reads on the WS path (point 4); the only field checked is hello.Token (server.go:513, via message.go:22), which the sim leaves empty in the hello body (point 3). Net: hello.Token == "" for every sim node → tokenOK == false always. "Supply valid tokens" cannot work against this code as written.

FIX DIRECTION (for the sibling children, not asserted here): the real fix is on the token/supply side — either provision the sim's token into the hello body field `token` (so hello.Token is populated and validator() can run), or make the mothership read the X-Spaxel-Token header on the WS upgrade and feed it to the validator. Tightening/widening the migration window is NOT a fix — it only moves when REJECT fires.

Source state: all citations verified clean against committed HEAD 402bb7b (none of the cited files carry uncommitted edits). Cross-references: PROGRESS.md bf-3hji (no-reject observation), bf-2hdbg (migration-window-is-the-mask), bf-29wyl (dead header path), bf-5ig3e (validator checks hello.Token only).

Both log "Node <MAC> rejected: missing token" then sendReject(conn,"invalid_token") (server.go:525; def server.go:841+) and conn.Close().

REQUIRED EXPLICIT STATEMENTS (acceptance criteria — re-affirmed):
- Current "no reject" is a 24h-window MASK, not a real fix: it vanishes the instant SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0 is set, or after 24h of uptime. The hardware-free build is green by accident of uptime, not by design.
- The token-supply path is currently DEAD: header ignored (no WS-path read), hello.Token empty (sim hello bodies omit it), tokenOK always false (server.go:513).

Authoritative write-up + fix options A/B/C: docs/notes/token-reject-root-cause.md. Closing bf-34lwt. The remaining bf-4iewr children should target point 4 (make the token contract real: either populate hello.Token in the sim body, or read X-Spaxel-Token in HandleNodeWS) rather than re-opening/lengthening the window, which only hides the bug.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Re-verified at HEAD on redispatch (umbrella/split-child, failure-count:2). This bead is stuck-open-but-done — the fix and its verification were already committed and pushed.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): validator wired UNCONDITIONALLY at main.go:4494 (ingestSrv.SetTokenValidator(provSrv.ValidateToken)). Migration window only opens if SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS>0 (main.go:4495-4499). Under strict (window=0, deadline.IsZero()), every tokenless node fell through to server.go:543-551 sendReject('invalid_token') + disconnect.

RESOLUTION (option 3 of scope — sim supplies valid tokens, already at HEAD):
- bf-1o7qi (server.go:524-528): bridge X-Spaxel-Token WS header into hello.Token so header-only clients are not tokenless to the validator.
- bf-4mle6 (cmd/sim/main.go:153, resolveTokens): spaxel-sim provisions a REAL per-node HMAC token via /api/provision by default. A valid token is accepted+paired REGARDLESS of window (server.go:537).

RE-VERIFICATION (fresh build from HEAD 51636ff, go1.25):
- go vet ./... clean; go test ./internal/ingestion/... green (fresh -count=1).
- Positive (window_test.sh, 4 nodes + 1 walker, default --provision):
  - STRICT (SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0): all 4 nodes provision real HMAC tokens, connect, receive roles, stream @240fps. mothership+sim stderr: 0 occurrences of reject/REJECT/invalid_token/401/403. PASS.
  - DEFAULT (24): same — PASS, sim-neg=0 ms-neg=0.
  (window_test.sh's LARGE=8760 arm fails only because 8760 exceeds the [0,168] config range — config validation rejects the VALUE, not a node. Test-data bug in bf-ekr9c's harness, not a product defect; STRICT is the bf-4iewr hard gate and it passes.)
- Negative control (STRICT, --token bogus, 2 nodes): sim rc=1; mothership logged '[WARN] Node ... rejected: invalid token' for both; sim received {type:reject,reason:invalid_token}. Validator LIVE and enforcing.

All 3 acceptance criteria hold. No new code change required; STRICT (bf-4iewr scenario) and DEFAULT are clean and the validator is provably still enforcing. Closing.

title: Make tokenless-sim acceptance durable for the hardware-free build
description:
Third link of the bf-4iewr split (depends on the token-bridge fix). Today the hardware-free sim is accepted only because MigrationWindowHours defaults to 24h — a value that silently EXPIRES (uptime > 24h) and that any `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0` boot disables. Make the HW-free build's auth policy explicit and durable so the sim never regresses.

## Scope
Decide and implement the supported policy for the hardware-free build (justify the choice; update PROGRESS.md "Hardware-Free Simulator -> CSI Streaming" section):
- (A) Sim ALWAYS supplies a valid token (now functional via the previous child) -> window-independent acceptance. Verify provisionToken() succeeds against /api/provision and the token validates; OR
- (B) E2E harness / documented boot sets the migration window EXPLICITLY and asserts it is open (no reliance on the implicit 24h default); OR
- (C) A build/test gate that leaves the validator unwired for the sim path.

Ensure the e2e harness (`mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go`, TestHarness.Start) and the PROGRESS.md human-run commands reflect the chosen policy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A documented, reproducible auth policy for the HW-free build in PROGRESS.md.
- [ ] Behavior is IDENTICAL whether SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS is 0, default (24), or large — sim nodes connect and stream in all three (i.e., no implicit dependence on the 24h default).
- [ ] E2E harness no longer silently relies on the implicit 24h window.

title: Wire fusion engine SetNodePosition — nodes never positioned, sim/IO-6 produces 0 blobs
description:
Found during investigation of bf-3v39 (presence-detection verification).

SYMPTOM: E2E tests (tests/e2e TestFullE2EIntegration, TestDetectionEvents) and the IO-6 hard-gate scenario produce ZERO detection events / blobs even with 4 nodes + 2 walkers running spaxel-sim. The fusion loop runs at 10Hz but never emits a peak.

ROOT CAUSE: internal/fusion/fusion.go Engine.SetNodePosition(mac,x,y,z) is defined but NEVER CALLED in the non-test wiring (grep shows only the definition + unrelated SetPositionProvider helpers). Nodes default to pos=(0,0,1) in the DB (internal/db/migrations.go:143-145). With all nodes co-located, the Fresnel zone excess path length |P-T|+|P-R|-|T-R| collapses to ~0 everywhere, so the accumulation grid has no meaningful peaks and confidence is undefined -> no blobs.

WHY IT MATTERS: This breaks the IO-6 release hard-gate ('walker produces a tracked blob') and means the eventual physical presence-detection validation (bf-3v39) will also show no blobs regardless of hardware, until node positions feed the fusion engine.

LIKELY FIX: In cmd/mothership/main.go, call fusion Engine.SetNodePosition for each node (a) at startup from the DB nodes table, and (b) whenever a node's position changes (PATCH /api/nodes/:mac position handler) or a node connects/registers. The spaxel-sim default node placement (createVirtualNodes) and/or a default zone may also need to seed positions so the simulator path exercises real geometry. Verify by re-running TestFullE2EIntegration and asserting len(blobs) > 0 during the run (the test currently ducks this with 'we verify the API responds correctly rather than asserting a minimum count').

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
bf-f0jee — IO-6 hard-gate test code is CLAIMED for bf-4q5w (fusion Engine.SetNodePosition wiring / IO-6 hard gate). Five functions, all in mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go:

1. TestIO6HardGate_WalkerProducesTrackedBlob — the strict release hard-gate; deliberately RED until bf-4q5w lands (only t.Skip in testing.Short()).
2. CaptureIO6Diagnostics — diagnostic-evidence capture for the triage flow.
3. GetNodeRecords — TestHarness helper: raw node records incl. persisted positions.
4. GetStatus — TestHarness helper: /api/status snapshot.
5. io6RunMaxima — concurrent blob count + detection-event count for diagnostics.

WORKING-TREE FATE DECISION: these five functions (~217 lines + the "strings" import they require) are UNCOMMITTED in the working tree, pending the bf-4q5w SetNodePosition fix. DECISION: LEAVE UNCOMMITTED and fold them into the bf-4q5w fix commit (the same change that wires Engine.SetNodePosition in cmd/mothership/main.go), so the deliberately-strict gate goes GREEN in the same commit that makes it pass. Rationale: the gate is a strict triage assertion expected RED until the fix lands, and cd mothership && go test ./... is a required pre-close gate — committing a known-RED gate ahead of the fix provides no value on its own (its helpers are consumed only by this gate) and risks turning the e2e gate red against an unfixed tree. They must NOT be folded into bf-qzrmq or bf-4mle6.

EXCLUSION VERIFIED: the IO-6 code is confirmed NOT in the bf-qzrmq commit (250056c). 'git show 250056c -- mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go' adds exactly 17 lines — a single comment block documenting the window policy + the SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0 env var (concern A). NONE of the five functions appear in the added code (their names appear only inside the commit MESSAGE, which documents the exclusion). Committed file = 1355 lines; working tree = 1572 lines — the 217-line delta is the IO-6 code held back here. This satisfies bf-qzrmq acceptance criterion #2 (IO-6 hard-gate test code separated from that bead and tracked on bf-4q5w) and keeps bf-4mle6 (tokenless-sim auth policy) ungated by IO-6.

title: GET /api/occupancy never resolves BLE-identified person names (always empty)
description:
GET /api/occupancy (mothership/internal/api/status.go, getOccupancy handler at line 122, TODO at line 151) always returns an empty "people" array for every zone: `People: []string{}, // TODO: resolve blob IDs to person labels`. It never converts occupied blob IDs to BLE-identified person names, even though BLE-to-blob identity matching (Component 21/Component 9 in docs/plan/plan.md, implemented in mothership/internal/ble -- see IdentityMatch.PersonName in internal/ble/identity.go) is already built and populates person names elsewhere (e.g. the dashboard snapshot's blobs[].person field, plan.md ~line 903-904 and ~908-909 "people": ["Alice"]). The plan's documented occupancy/zone snapshot explicitly includes real names, and mothership/internal/api/status.go's own doc comment on occupancyResponse says "people: List of person names (BLE-identified) in the zone" -- but the implementation never does this lookup, so /api/occupancy (and anything consuming it, e.g. Simple Mode room cards per plan.md Component 8d ~line 1010 "Shows occupancy count, person names... and status color") is silently wrong: it will always show 0 named people even when BLE identity is confidently resolved.

Root closed bead bf-59me3 ("GET /api/status and GET /api/occupancy endpoints") shipped the endpoint shape but left this specific resolution as a stub; no open bead currently tracks finishing it.

Fix: in getOccupancy (mothership/internal/api/status.go), wire an identity-resolution provider (mirroring how other subsystems already consume mothership/internal/ble's identity matches/tracker -- grep for existing consumers of ble.IdentityMatch or a "identity provider" interface already wired into StatusHandler's siblings, e.g. how internal/analytics/alert_handler.go or the dashboard snapshot builder gets person names for blobs) so that for each zone, People is populated with the PersonName (or Label(), once bf-5mx46 lands) of each BLE-identified blob currently inside that zone, falling back to omitting unidentified blobs from the People list (they still count toward Count). Add/extend a unit test in mothership/internal/api/status_test.go covering: a zone with one BLE-identified occupant returns that person's name in People; a zone with an unidentified blob returns Count>0 but People=[]. Verify with: cd ~/spaxel/mothership && go test ./internal/api/... -run Occupancy -v.

Closed by claude-code-glm-5-alpha.

## Summary

Added the three canonical identity fields (PersonName / AssignedColor / IdentityResolved) to every in-scope blob-creation site, per the bf-1q3m consolidated inventory. The fields surface as undefined (omitted) for existing blobs.

### Field definitions (canonical, camelCase JSON keys match dashboard/types/spaxel.d.ts)
- PersonName  string  json:"personName,omitempty"        ("" = unresolved)
- AssignedColor string json:"assignedColor,omitempty"    ("" = unresolved)
- IdentityResolved *bool json:"identityResolved,omitempty" (tri-state: nil=unattempted, &true=resolved, &false=failed)

### In-scope types now carrying the 3 fields (8 total, verified at HEAD d0ac1b2)
Primary (added a612584): signal.TrackedBlob, tracker.Blob (3D), tracking.Blob (2D), automation.TrackedBlob.
Projections (added 01a415d): api.Track (/api/tracks), dashboard.blobJSON (/ws/dashboard). /api/blobs needs no projection (serializes signal.TrackedBlob directly).
Tier-1 leak targets (added c3f4b0d / 29a114c + 7309564): explainability.BlobSnapshot, volume.BlobPos.

### Construction sites — all 21 production sites accounted for
5 primary + 16 projection = 21. The 6 in-scope sites' types carry the fields (left at Go zero values at construction → omitempty → undefined in JS). The 15 out-of-scope sites are enumerated with reasons in notes/bf-5151-coverage.md (pre-identity Fresnel peak C1; synthetic sim/replay P8-P10; anon-struct/lifecycle/trigger-state sites; rendering fallbacks; dead api.BlobPos). Zero sites silently dropped.

### Tests added (this commit d0ac1b2)
Table-driven serialization guards on the 3 types whose fields are reachable via JSON: signal/identity_fields_test.go, automation/identity_fields_test.go, api/tracks_identity_test.go. Each asserts (1) zero-value blobs omit all three (undefined), (2) resolved identity emits camelCase keys, (3) *bool tri-state: &false still emits identityResolved:false.

### Gate
gofmt clean; go vet ./... exit 0; go test ./... 50 packages ok, 0 failures.

Population from the BLE identity sidecar (ble.IdentityMatcher.GetMatch) is intentionally a follow-up bead — this one only adds the type surface as undefined, exactly as scoped.

title: Observe at least one tracked blob at runtime via /api/blobs
description:
Decomposed from bf-40hc. Third link: with sim streaming CSI (child bf-3hji), observe at least one tracked blob at runtime.

The e2e harness already proves this works — bf-2aqf hard-asserts >=1 blob via AssertBlobObserved in mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go. This child reproduces that observation OUTSIDE the test (manual run).

## Scope
- While spaxel-sim streams, poll GET http://localhost:8080/api/blobs (returns a JSON array of tracked blobs) over the run window; track the peak count.
- /ws/dashboard (snapshot/delta messages carrying a blobs array) is an acceptable alternative observation channel.
- If no blob appears within ~30s, tune sim params (walkers/rate/duration) until one does and record the working values.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least one blob observed via /api/blobs (or /ws/dashboard) during a run.
- [ ] Working sim parameters (nodes/walkers/rate/duration/seed) recorded so the result is reproducible.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
## Verdict: NOT splittable — blocked upstream, not by scope. Refusing the auto-split.

Ground-truth investigation shows this bead fails for a single upstream reason, not because it's too big. A prior splittability check already returned {"splittable": false}. Splitting would spawn children all blocked by the same defect.

### What I found (auto-split premise is false)

The bead premise says "the e2e harness already proves this works — bf-2aqf hard-asserts >=1 blob." It does NOT — that harness is RED:

- `TestFullE2EIntegration` FAILS at HEAD (5210cae): `AssertDuringRun: no blobs or detection events observed during 30s run window (max concurrent blobs: 0, detection events: 0): fusion pipeline produced no output`.
- Same test FAILS at 3b00ccf — the very commit that ADDED the >=1-blob assertion. So blob production from the sim has never worked in this path; the regression predates the assertion.

In all runs the sim streams fine (240 fps, all 4 nodes connect, get roles, fleet coverage 0%→21%), but the fusion/tracker localizes **0 blobs**. Nodes online ✓; blob production ✗.

### Two real script bugs in blob_observation.sh (fixed in working tree)

1. `--duration "${SIM_DURATION}s"` — the sim's `-duration` flag is an **int (seconds)**, not a duration string. `40s` → `invalid value "40s" for flag -duration: parse error` → the sim **exited immediately and never connected** (root cause of the 0-nodes/0-blobs in prior runs). Fixed to `--duration "$SIM_DURATION"`. The e2e harness itself documents this exact gotcha (e2e_test.go:196).
2. `nodes_online` jq filter `select(.status=="online")` always returns 0 — `/api/nodes` has **no `status` field** (uses role/last_seen_at). Cosmetic only; nodes do connect.

### Confound cleaned up
Stale `/tmp/spaxel-mothership` processes (PIDs 1918338/1926275, uptime ~43min) were squatting on port 8080, so earlier e2e runs hit the squatter (`uptime: 2537s`) instead of their own process. Killed; clean re-run (uptime 0s, fresh build) still fails — so pollution is NOT the cause.

### Recommendation
This bead is genuinely blocked on the fusion/sim-CSI→blob pipeline producing output. Options:
(a) Fix the fusion pipeline (separate focused bead — likely the real work), then this bead is trivially completable (script already fixed; just run it and record params).
(b) If blobs are known to require real hardware CSI (not synthetic sim), re-scope the bead/parent accordingly.

Not closing (criteria unmet: 0 blobs). Not splitting (single blocker, not scope).

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Split into the real blocker chain (3 children), parent -> umbrella. NOT a mechanical slice of the observation task.

## Re-verified the blocker today (2026-07-07)
Ran blob_observation.sh with fresh builds on port 8088: spaxel-sim streams at 240 FPS, all 4 nodes connect + get roles, fleet coverage 0->21%, ZERO REJECTs (reject_in_sim_log=0). BUT peak_blob_count=0 over 40s. Same as comment [142]. The e2e TestFullE2EIntegration agrees (max concurrent blobs: 0).

The bead is NOT too big — it is blocked by a single upstream defect. So comment [142] correctly refused a scope-split. But refusing again just kicks the auto-split to a 5th firing. Instead I decomposed the ACTUAL blocker chain ([142] option a):

1. bf-20q9o — Diagnose why runtime fusion emits 0 blobs from sim CSI (investigation; deliverable = root cause). Located the defect region: runtime bridge `result := fusionEngine.Fuse(gatherFusionLinks(pm)); blobs := blobTracker.track(result); pm.SetTrackedBlobs(blobs)` (cmd/mothership/main.go ~line 2056). The tracker works in unit tests (tracker_test.go Update -> 1 blob), so Fuse() is returning 0 peaks from the sim's link data. Pin the exact gate.
2. bf-2eub9 — Fix runtime CSI->blob pipeline to emit >=1 blob from spaxel-sim CSI (blocked by bf-20q9o).
3. bf-16mw1 — Observe >=1 blob via /api/blobs + record reproducible sim params (blocked by bf-2eub9). This child IS the original bf-51fq acceptance, now achievable once the pipeline emits blobs.

Parent depends on bf-16mw1. Children are sequential. blob_observation.sh is already corrected (int-seconds duration, port 8088) and left in the working tree for child 3 to use.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
## Refused auto-split (4th firing): bead is ALREADY split AND verified complete.

The dispatch premise ('failed 3×, needs breaking down') is empirically false:

1. **Already decomposed** (comment [143]): 3 children created and all CLOSED — bf-20q9o (diagnose), bf-2eub9 (fix pipeline), bf-16mw1 (observe). Parent already carries the umbrella label and depends on bf-16mw1.
2. **The 'failures' were a flaky param, not scope**: the 0-blob mystery (comments [142]/[143]) was resolved by SIM_RATE 20->30 — the [[spaxel-sim-verify-mode-blob-check]] fix. No fusion-pipeline defect remained.

### Re-verified at runtime just now (HEAD 5bbe52c == predispatch sha)
Ran ./blob_observation.sh (builds canonical mothership/cmd/sim from source, streams CSI, polls GET /api/blobs, cross-checks sim --verify):
- peak_blob_count=2 (>=1 blob via /api/blobs)  -> criterion 1 MET
- first_blob_at=1s, 25 samples, 4 nodes online throughout, 0 rejects, ~363 FPS
- sim --verify PASSED: '[SIM] PASS: 2 blobs detected for 2 walkers'
- Working params recorded (reproducible): nodes=4 walkers=2 rate=30 duration=25 seed=42 -> criterion 2 MET

Both acceptance criteria satisfied. Splitting again would spawn a redundant duplicate child chain on top of the closed one. Closing as complete (no --body per [[auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid]]).

title: Add the IO-6 hard-gate scenario test and a strict block-back triage gate
description:
Split from bf-5jeo (verification capstone). Depends on the TestFullE2EIntegration child. Child 4 of 4 (final, blocks the parent).

CONTEXT: The IO-6 release hard-gate is "walker produces a tracked blob" with 4 nodes + 2 walkers running spaxel-sim (the config described in bf-4q5w; see also internal/signal/processor.go:607,626 which already documents that no engine feeds the live blob loop). There is currently no dedicated test that exercises this gate end-to-end.

SCOPE:
- Add a dedicated IO-6 hard-gate scenario (a tagged e2e test or runbook+test) that runs RunSimulator(ctx, 4 nodes, 2 walkers, 20 rate, duration) and asserts a walker produces a tracked blob end-to-end, reusing the assertion helpers from bf-2330.
- CRITICAL TRIAGE GATE: if blobs are still zero after upstream bf-4q5w lands, DO NOT weaken the assertion. Instead: (a) keep this child blocked, (b) capture a finding as a comment with diagnostic evidence (fusion accumulation grid state, node positions, traces under .beads/traces/), and (c) feed that finding back to upstream bf-4q5w (fusion Engine.SetNodePosition wiring) rather than weakening the assertion.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- An IO-6 hard-gate scenario exists that asserts a tracked blob is produced with 4 nodes + 2 walkers running spaxel-sim.
- When green: the IO-6 release hard-gate (walker produces a tracked blob) is satisfied end-to-end.
- When zero blobs: the assertion stays strict, and a finding with trace evidence is filed back to bf-4q5w instead of weakening — never silently degrade this test.
- bf-5jeo (this capstone umbrella) cannot close until this child is green.
